I am doing the Hcg with the CT. Not adding in the CO. Just not eating the fruit or the grains this round. My losses are about the same as when I did the protocol last year. I had a major slip up though last week that I think if I didn't have, my losses would be much more. Somedays I am losing more than a pound, but averaging overall .5 a day. I am on day 21.

One thing that may be affecting my losses. My dad is doing really bad right now this last week. He is battling lung/brain/pelvic cancer. So I am thinking the emotional stress and anxiety may be detrimental to good losses also. I will try to keep going, but if things get too stressful, I may have to end the round to deal with what I need to deal with and go right into P3 with just LRx/paleo eating until I can get things back on an even keel. ### shilohman — 2012-04-01T06:59:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-9706>

> **rocketmom;9753 wrote:** Bumping to see if anyone has some suggestions...

You have to lose the chicken from your diet. Chickens are fed corn/soy pellets, so their meat is very high in omega 6. Have you had your omega 6/3 ratio tested? As long as you are eating any food high in omega 6 you are going to gain weight. This is why Dr K recommends only red grass fed meat.

### Honey Crisp — 2012-04-01T12:54:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-9729>

> **rocketmom;9753 wrote:** ...I am perplexed to say the least. I am stubborn and refuse to give up.
>
>
>
> Any insight (or even a hang in there!) is greatly appreciated.

What type of diet were you eating before protocol? If it was a paleo/primal I don't think you'll see a big drop from water weight like you do going from SAD to Hcg Protocol.

How much weight do you need/want to lose? I'm in my healthy weight range and the losses are usually slow for me at this point.

I think you did a great job in ditching the Walden Farm products. It's frankenfood. You are healing your body with good food and adding the frankenfood cancels it.

### texasmom4 — 2012-04-01T15:37:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-9744>

> **shilohman;9768 wrote:** You have to lose the chicken from your diet. Chickens are fed corn/soy pellets, so their meat is very high in omega 6. Have you had your omega 6/3 ratio tested? As long as you are eating any food high in omega 6 you are going to gain weight. This is why Dr K recommends only red grass fed meat.

Chicken is "on protocol" per Dr Simeon, but Shilohman has a good point if you are trying to marry Dr K's advice with HCG. FWIW, I did gain/stall out every time I ate chicken during round 1 and 2. Never connected the dots until I started round 3 though. I only ate deer meat, fish, and an occasional beef sirloin my entire round 3 and had really great losses.

Have you scrutinized all of your facial products/make-up? I lost less when I weighed Monday mornings and finally figured out my "oil free" foundation that I put on for church on Sunday was the problem.

I'm sorry you are having trouble. At least you aren't hungry - that would indicate a big problem. August may be right - you may just have to ride it out. I hope the scale starts moving for you soon!

Amanda

### smwpeterson@gmail.com — 2012-04-01T16:23:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-9761>

I did it! I took the challenge and I lost. On Friday I had not planned well for a shopping trip and found myself at a Mexican restaurant hungry. I order a house salad with dressing on the side and my husband had fajita. I took some of the fajita chicken and onions from him and “dipped” a little dressing. I also could not resist a few chips and salsa. Then what the heck a square of dark chocolate. The rest of the day was as Simeon instructions.

I lost 1.6 lbs wow I thought I had blown it.

Saturday I had bacon for breakfast 40min in my pool at 70 degrees. Regular Dr. S rest of day except the on dark chocolate square.

Then 25 min in tub at 60 degree last night. This morning I lost 1 lb. I ate more protein and fat yesterday and did not experience the hunger that I have been fighting while on Simeon protocol.

I have lost 7.8 lbs. in 7 days that includes my load weight.

Just had my bacon headed to cold tub and will grill grass fed beef today. Can we do the BAB on this? Dare I try?

Anyone else trying fat? I sure had missed bacon and could see my skin was getting very dry.

I will update you as I go.

### indigogirl — 2012-04-01T17:01:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-9769>

I am eating bacon for bkfst...40% fat reduced and losing really well. Ate this bacon on my first round and also lost really well.

I also use a Clinique eye cream day and night and a dermalogica lotion to calm my roseacea in the morning.

My hands are very dry from the lack of fat and I use a hand lotion frequently throughout the day.

Otherwise following Simeon's protocol.

I am CTing very sporadically (face dunks) and would like to increase this, but am traveling a lot in April and will see how I can fit it in.

I'm on day 23 and have lost 16.5 lbs, and plan on staying on HCG for 40 days.

I eat chicken, but only from Whole Foods...which is organic, but I don't think it's pastured.

I will stabilize slowly over a course of 6 weeks, and last time I continued to lose weight over stabilization, about another 6 lbs. For a total of 42 lbs. During stabilization I still didn't eat carbs and only low sugar fruits (berries, apples, grapefruit). I did eat eggs, yogurt, and nuts. Might not add back eggs this time (I have hashimotos), but think it will be really hard to not eat eggs due to their convenience.

I maintained my weight for about 6 months, started Paleo, tried the Leptin Reset twice and each time cheated and ended up completely blowing everything, ate crap and gained everything back over about 6 months.

I think we are all so individual!

I look forward to following everyone's experiments.

### rocketmom — 2012-04-01T19:13:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-9831>

Thanks everyone for such great input. I really appreciate all the info.

I am 59 so TOM is not an issues. I did stabilize on P3/P4 using Paleo/LR. More paleo than LR since the BAB was difficult for me. I realize that I haven't been eating grains, legumes, sugar, starch and lots of dairy since I started Jan 12 on hCG, so my great loss in week one of vlcd was the elimination of those food groups. I do buy chicken at the local grocery and they probably are fed grain etc. I buy organic grass fed beef but have shied away from beef my first week. Weighing in this am I am down 3.5 lbs and that averages to .5 which is what I am aiming for this 40 day round. I have yet to start CT but the more I read about it the better it looks, too bad I am such a coward! Cabbage being a reason for stalling breaks my heart, as I used it alot through all the phases of R1. I tossed all the Walden Farms stuff(when hubby was out walking the dogs...just what he needed to see...wasting $...) I will get my chicken from Sprouts where I get the organic grass fed beef tomorrow.

I will attempt to do CT this evening. We are on our way to South Padre Island to celebrate my FIL's 95th birthday. We will be eating out...not me that is. But they do have boiled peel and eat shrimp and salad so I may be able to finagle something but will bring my cooler bag with me just in case. I will bring my own homemade salad dressing too.

### colleencoble — 2012-04-02T00:42:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-10006>

The bummer is that hcg has gotten a little tougher to get than it was 2 mos ago. But you can still get it at [www.reliablerx.com](http://http://www.reliablerx.com). You need either 1 5000 iu vial (could just barely squeak through with this on a 30 day round) or better yet 2 2000 iu vials. You can get the bac water on Amazon. You'll need a mixing needle for each vial. I buy the 18 gauge. And you'll need insulin needles which you can get at gpzservices.com. If you buy 2000 iu vials you won't need sterile mixing vials--you can add the vac water to the hcg vial.

I did all my rounds while on the Dzugan method. It really doesn't matter whether you're on it or not. Hcg is sooo great! It works better than you can imagine.  If you go to my website ([www.colleencoble.com](http://http://www.colleencoble.com)) go to Extras and there is a page for hcg. There are links there for the syringes. The links for hcg don't work now because All Day Chemist isn't selling it. Any of the hcg brands are good.

You need either 1 5000 iu vial (could just barely squeak through with this on a 30 day round) or better yet 2 2000 iu vials. You can get the bac water on Amazon. You'll need a mixing needle for each vial. I buy the 18 gauge. And you'll need insulin needles which you can get at gpzservices.com. If you buy 2000 iu vials you won't need sterile mixing vials--you can add the vac water to the hcg vial.
>
>
>
> I did all my rounds while on the Dzugan method. It really doesn't matter whether you're on it or not. Hcg is sooo great! It works better than you can imagine.  If you go to my website ([www.colleencoble.com](http://http://www.colleencoble.com)) go to Extras and there is a page for hcg. There are links there for the syringes. The links for hcg don't work now because All Day Chemist isn't selling it. Any of the hcg brands are good.

thanks as always colleen!

### colleencoble — 2012-04-02T03:47:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-10097>

You don't need mixing vials if you get the 2000 iu. But yes you will need the syringes to go with the needles.  and bac water and needles look good.

### rbu5@sssnet.com — 2012-04-02T03:51:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-10099>

> **nuttmegs17;10148 wrote:** so everything else looks ok?
>
>
>
> colleen said: "If you buy 2000 iu vials you won't need sterile mixing vials--you can add the vac water to the hcg vial. "
>
>
>
> is that not true?
>
>
>
>
>
> thank you!

Oh sorry, I see you are mixing the 2000iu. So if you are dosing 125iu/day (which is what Dr S protocol recommends) you will need 8cc of bac water to mix. That should give you 16 days. You will need the dispensing vials....[at least the 10cc size](http://http://www.gpzservices.com/items/products/vials%7Epresealed/-br%7Ebr-amber/10-ml-presealed-vial-amber-10mlap-detail.htm). You will add 1cc of bac water into the vial with the hcg pill to dissolve. Then use the syringe to take the 1cc dissolved hcg and gently add to the dispenser amber vial, then add your remaining 7cc of bac water to get your 8cc total. That is what you will draw from everyday.

Connie will chime in if need to correct this.

### Shinegirl — 2012-04-02T16:13:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-10280>

Good morning everyone! I hope everyone is feeling amazing today. I only got 5 hrs sleep but when I did close my eyes I was completely out. Slept so well. I'm still a little tired though. So, funny story. Kinda.

I was determined to get my CT yesterday after my lunch and dinner but I had to work around a few challenges. I was vacationing with my in-laws and my husbands mother has MS. She drinks iced tea like crazy so was not about to steal all her ice for my CT bath.

Cold shower is just not enough for me so I decided to try something that I thought was pretty clever.

Everyone knows what thermocline is right?

Well, I'm in Texas, so the 1st foot of our lake is pretty much lukewarm water right now. However, if you reach down about 2 1/2 ft you can stir up some 55 - 60 degree water. Perfect for growing fishies like me.

So long story short... I asked my husband how ling I was out there swimming underwater. My goal was 25 to 30 minutes, which is my average CT bath. Well, he told me I swam for an hour and a half which is ridiculous because I'm such a "floater".

Long story shorter:

I gained .4 lbs

I feel incredibly balanced. This mornings yoga was a breeze. No tightness.

Slept well

Lost 1/2 inch off waist

Lunch: 1 can tuna with egg and mayo on sliced zuccini

Coco manna

Dinner: 1 chicken breast salad with 1 egg and mayo

Coco manna

Coco manna

### Marilee0123 — 2012-04-02T17:26:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-10305>

Hello everyone! I thought I would jump in here and join you. I'm on R2P2D43 of Hcg with a loss of 33.8lbs. That is a typical loss for me for 40 days. I follow Simeon's protocol. For the past 2 weeks I've incorporated CT. My losses on week 4 (1st week of CT showed a gain of 2lbs) NEVER had a weekly gain on Hcg. On week 5 I has a total .8 loss for the week. I dropped the fruit (strawberries), continued the CT and had a 4lb loss in one day. I am continuing to drop steady as I normally do. Here's some background info on me....I found Hcg in Feb 2011. I started at 335lbs, injected continually for 7 months from the end Feb '11 until the end of Sept '11. I showed no immunity and I took a few PI's (2-3) for myself and my family. In Sept my LIW was 205. I maintained until the end of Dec and I started to slowly gain 10 lbs without changing my eating habits of maintenance. I could not correct back to 205 and stay there. It was very discouraging. I found LeptinRx in Jan and I was able to maintain at 215. I started this round on Feb 20 at 235. That included a 20lb load weight...ugh...which came off the first few days, whew! Today I'm sitting at 198.4. I will continue like I did last time until I reach a weight that I feel comfortable with (still haven't decided what that should be!). I belong to all the BIG boards and even after a year or more, I continue to read and learn something new everyday. I felt that I could hopefully help someone else and also benefit myself the most from this forum. Happy Losing!

Marilee

### challdo — 2012-04-02T19:03:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-10369>

> **RBee;10402 wrote:** oh, and gym pants....with my sweatshirt LOL

Rbee-clothes are optional only in some areas of the world!

Hey everyone,

I am 52yo female who is a practioner and patient of hCG. I have over 1000 patients who have done the diet. P3/4 throws everyone for a loop and now I am asking all to do Leptin Rx for phase 3/4.

My last round (#?) I did Leptin Rx for Phase 3/4 and stablized like a rock but never lost weight on it or on post Leptin protocol. Been doing CT 4-7 days a week in 48 degree water for a 1/2 hour or more.

Yesterday I started again-a 20 day round-I will modify this with Keto diet-meat only with breakfast and dinner. I will do all of the supplements Doc has indicated. I am still debating adding coconut oil/MCT.

Will report stats in the a.m. as to post load numbers.

I'm tired of this and want to get my weight down for good. Leptin resistance is the missing link. Lifestyle modification is required and will be embraced.

Watched my labs-hsCRP was elevated immediately post hCG Phase 2-then was able to cut it in half with supplements and Leptin Rx but couldnt get it lower. Thyroid ratio was 80! Boy, was I happy to see this as I take iodine and Armour thyroid daily. DHEAS was >200. Pregnenalone was low. Currently taking progesterone sublingual, Pregnenalone, DHEA, Testosterone. No Estrogen as still having periods. Taking almost all supplements for Leptin Rx too-but these stop tomorrow as I start hCG VLCD in a.m. and will switch to those suggested by Dr Kruse.

Also, I agree with Jack, hCG works best for the obese patient. Losses of 25-34 lbs are common on a 40 day round in my practice. YMMV-(your mileage may vary)

Looking for support as this is very off protocol, and like Colleen, I was very Dr.S. indoctrinated.

Cindy

### colleencoble — 2012-04-02T19:39:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-10385>

The Corion vial is actually a little larger then the hucog vial. And both will allow a total of 4 cc of fluid in a 2000 iu vial which is a good mixing dose.  You can't do it with a 5000 iu vial though. I don't like a super potent mix.

Colleen

### MaggieC — 2012-04-02T20:51:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-10420>

Welcome Marilee and Cindy! The more the merrier! I'm doing shots at 150 mg per day, (all previous rounds were 200 mg) and I keep mine in the freezer until I need them. I came down with this plague at the same time I started vlcd, so my losses are not stellar. I'm sure they will improve once I start feeling better. Been running a fever for 3 days now and I'm ready to be done with this! It doesn't help that I forgot my shot yesterday. Grrr. Calories staying at 500 and my percentages are around 65% protein, 20% fat, and 15% carbs. The only fruit I eat are raspberries, I've turned them into my guilty pleasure. If I splurge on those, I might get in about 30 calories!

### erika8683 — 2012-04-02T21:01:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-10427>

I started the Leptin RX last Monday, I had some left over hcg from last year and decided to take it in addition the eating changes. I only use it once a day (10 drops) instead of the recommended 2-3X a day, I find I loose more weight on the lower dose. I take a tablespoon of coconut oil every morning, even though hcg instructions prohibit fat intake! In seven days, I've lost seven pounds and I haven't been completely Paleo, although unintentionally- I've had re fried beans and cheese for lunch daily and a couple of nights I've had peanuts for dinner.

All things considered, I view this as a success. I'll be cutting out the non-paleo items this week and see if the results are any better!

Have my fingers crossed!

do any of you have a suggested meal plan?

my plan is to do 500 calories

eat breakfast and/or lunch dinner

no snacks (a la LR)

### rocketmom — 2012-04-02T21:51:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-10447>

Welcome to all the newbies. This is a fabulous group. I was having a problem in my current second round and everyone jumped in to help. I love the diversity of the members here...and reading about all the experiences and tweeks everyone is doing to maximize the hCG and LRx and CT!

Marilee...you are amazing! Congrats on such a successful hCG experience. You are an inspiration.

My first week loss on R2P2 is 5 lbs. I'm averaging a bit over .5 per day which is right on target. I was really starting to worry, but I knew that I would catch up at some point. I also realize that round 1 is usually great losses, and coming from Paleo/LRx I should have prepared myself for lower losses. It is so great when you see great losses, that sometimes you just gotta lower your expectations a tad to keep yourself sane!

I am mentally preparing for my CT. I need to get a pan or bowl 'cause my head/face doesn't fit into either bathroom sink with our new hardware, lol!

dennise

edited to note...my total loss for week one was 3.4...total 4.2 with todays weight. Good thing I have a great spreadsheet/tracker that works so well...my math is in the toilet.

### MaggieC — 2012-04-02T22:21:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-10464>

The trick to weighing in every day is to not get attached to the weight. Some weeks will move nicely, and some just won't. The theory is that the empty fat cells are filling up with water before they finally let go I believe... Someone on another forum is tracking her body composition numbers to prove that theory... it's fascinating stuff. Our bodies are complicated organisms that have to do things their way. Do your best to look at it from a scientific/experimental point of view and you'll be much happier. You can't beat yourself up because you aren't losing a pound a day! That's a waste of energy and a spike in your cortisol levels!

Weighing daily gives you good data if you're tracking your food intake. I use perfect diet tracker for instance. That can help you figure out if you have food sensitivities. It's all just data!

### colleencoble — 2012-04-02T23:10:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-10491>

I like weighing every day but it's not going to hurt if you just don't want to do that. But you need to weigh every day in P3 so you can take action with a steak day when it's needed.

Pounds and Inches says to skip an injection a week so you can go 40 SHOTS. If you skip a day a week, that stretches it to 47 days or so until you take your final injection.

### colleencoble — 2012-04-03T15:47:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-10744>

nutmeggs, I don't do anything fancy while on hcg so I can't really help you with menus. I eat very plainly so I can break the bond to food. Boredom is good while on the protocol. LOL I don't eat out if I can help it while on the protocol. Actually I don't eat out nearly as much as I used to in general because you just can't trust what they put in your food. If I do have to eat out, I order fish and broccoli because if a tiny bit of fat makes its way to the fish, it's still lower in fat and calories than any other choice.

Joanne, you might try just icing your back instead of submersion if you can't stand it. And I wear socks to bed. LOL

shinegirl, now is where the truth will come out about whether you will lose well with the modified diet. You can't trust the first week, especially with gaining a lot on load. That flies off in the first week and if you gained more than usual, that will account for higher than usual numbers. Hope the losses pick back up though! But the next two weeks will show us more than anything else will.

I always have 10 day stalls in the middle of my round. I'm a slow loser too, about .5 a day average. I'm hoping the elimination of fruit will help that but we'll see. Hcg is so tricky. LOL

### Shinegirl — 2012-04-04T14:45:37+0300

Not a good morning.

I don't know what to make of last night but I'm not willing to go through that again!

I got the same foggy/ dizzy warm feeling as the night before, same time. This time I had to visit the bathroom urgently think I was having possibly a BM - sorry but listen. Instead nausea set in and I began vomiting with intense heat throughout my body. No sweating or anything no pain. I thought stomach virus but then I felt just fine after. No fever.

All night I had periods of my body pulsating with heat- fire heat starting in my stomach and reaching to my head and toes. No pain no sweating, pretty comfortable I might add, and i have NEVER experienced anything like that in my whole life.

2 CT yesterday

High protein (seafood) low veggie, no fruit

Coco manna

Lots of water

Hmmm... What to do.

### rocketmom — 2012-04-05T04:34:02+0300

I guess I have planned this second round very poorly...FIL turned 95 on Saturday and we took him to South Padre Island for fresh fish, both my husband and I are celebrating birthdays today, and Easter is Sunday. I turned 59 today, and this is gonna be the last birthday I will acknowledge! I treated myself to a filet mignon for dinner. Since I have eliminated fruit this round I had a bigger filet than the 3.5 but stayed under my 500 calories. Not doing well on CT. I did not like the face dunking but I am doing the icing. Hanging in there. Averaging .5 loss per day so I am very satisfied.

dennise

### Zoe Patricia — 2012-04-05T15:46:34+0300

Happy Birthday Denise, Good for you icing and staying protocol. Keep celebrating those birthdays.

I was re reading the doctors comments on CT and detox. I was feeling like I had induction flu yesterday already. I am just starting to ice my belly while laying in cold tub and doing legs against the wall... I have skinny legs and big belly...

in a comment Dr K said shredding the belly fat released stores of old estrogen and other stuff that could make you feel wonky. Just in case this hcg with cold really works as awesome as we all hope, I thought it might be a good idea to put together a first aid kit of detox band aids.

Dr K said to hit the b vitamins hard.

I happened to have 2 rx b and c vitamin vials but when those are gone I am out of luck and so need to order something OTC. Any ideas?

Also he suggested bone broth (some where in there) but that is not really on protocol. The new Atkins book says broth clears up induction flu in a flash so I am soaking on it. Wish there was a way to make broth on protocol.

Laughter is supposed to help cortisol which really grabbed me. Definitely going in my first aid kit.

### vlynnb — 2012-04-05T18:30:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-11773>

Colleen, This may not be the place to post this but it seems you look at this thread a lot so I chose it. Would you tell again what you did for food sensitivities or tell me where to find the info? I know I read it somewhere but can't find it. I have many food allergies and need all the help I can get.

Started hhcg for vanity and then realized how much dropping wheat and milk did for my stuffy nose. I also have osteoporosis and Eosiniphilic Esophagitis I know LR Rx will help. I lost 28 pounds on two rounds and for the second round went into Leptin Rx for p3. I have continued to lose slowly, 5.6 pounds in 6 weeks. I also am doing the leaky gut Rx. but I am not sure how to know if one's leaky gut is healed.

I haven't started the CT. My mom died about the time I started P3 (a blessing as she suffered from osteoporosis and other neolithic disease that had completely debilitated her for years - but still a lot of stress and hectic schedule for me) which slowed down my progressing to CT. I also live in the sticks and am still waiting for the thermometer to arrive. Which reminds me, Colleen, where do you buy a laser for the desensitizing?

I have about 15 pounds to lose to get to a 19 BMI and as I am losing slowly still I am reticent to do CT. Will it hurt in your opinion to continue a keto diet without CT? Dr. K has said the vagus may be damaged in the Nissan procedure I had for reflux but as I am contining to lose I am hoping that is not so. I do have more lower body weight to lose than upper though so probably CT, especially lower body, would be helpful.

I appreciate anyone's opinions on these questions.

### rocketmom — 2012-04-05T20:46:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-11823>

> **Zoe Patricia;11772 wrote:**
>
> Also he suggested bone broth (some where in there) but that is not really on protocol. The new Atkins book says broth clears up induction flu in a flash so I am soaking on it. Wish there was a way to make broth on protocol.
>
> Laughter is supposed to help cortisol which really grabbed me. Definitely going in my first aid kit.

Patricia, I make my own bone broth from chicken bones. I buy the bone in skin on breasts and debone, defat and throw the skin away. I plop them babies in my crock pot, add some water, garlic powder, onion powder and maybe some celery tops if I have them and let it cook away. Usually overnight. It makes awesome stock. You can add salt to your liking. Much better than any low sodium stuff in a box or can. Strain out the bones and the little bit of chicken meat, and the celery tops and then stick in the fridge. Skim off any fat that remains to defat it. I think someone on another board mentioned that homemade stock was about 5 calories a cup. I also do beef bone stock, the same way. It rocks. Ask the meat guy for soup bones. There are some hcg recipes online about making chicken stock from your daily protein allowance, but I find that after cooking in water the chicken is really awful.

dennise

### MartiD — 2012-04-05T22:16:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-11867>

Ok, I have read all through this thread and wanted to stop in for a quick hello. My background is pretty easy, I started with round 1 last June and lost 28lbs, did the reset in between rounds and stayed about 2lbs under LDW. Did a second round in the fall, shorter round (24 day), immediately went in to LeptinRX mid October and then dropped another 10 lbs til I started CT in February. In the last month or so I have lost another 3 lbs. My original goal was 195 from 228lbs. After first round success I adjusted to 185 for second round. I finished round 2 at 187 and today I am at 173. I would like to now go to 160-165. This would put me in a very comfortable size 10 and even though according to the BMI chart, which I know doesn't have much credibility, I still would be consider a healthy weight but I would be happy!

So, I am debating two options, stick with LeptinRX plus CT and see what happens with the warmer months coming up or finish out a bottle of HHCG pellets I have from last fall, try to go at least 21 days and add CT, then immediately fall back into LeptinRX. I never had an issue with stabilizing, never went 2lbs over LDW either round and according to my labs last fall I am not out of whack on my system.

Thoughts? I guess my biggest fear is messing up a good thing. I know how lucky I am that I have dropped more after rounds on Leptin and CT. Am I being greedy?

### Bernadette — 2012-04-06T02:28:33+0300

Marilee, I'm super shocked that you are seeing gains... I sure hope someone has an answer for you on this.

I still haven't 100% abandoned the calorie consideration in dieting, though I don't believe it's as simple as a lot of folks make it seem, but generally speaking, 500 calories should result in a noticeable drop on the scale

I do find it a relief to not wake up every morning and immediately jump on the scale. It's helped my stress a lot, lol

ETA: I just had a thought... have you been doing CT for awhile? It's supposed to release hormones from your fat stores. If you have just started CT you may still be releasing lots of estrogen from your stored fat, and I can definitely see a gain with high estrogen levels...

Hello everyone! It's the start of Easter vacation for me and I too decided to forget the scale for a few days. As some of you know, I'm back on protocol with CT. ### Slone07 — 2012-04-06T16:45:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-12120>

I've been bitten by the hCG bug again (thanks to y'all).

My plan was to do the full leptin reset (no CT for me yet) to see where it brought me in eight to ten weeks (I'm at two and a half weeks), but with it being the start of summer here in TX and me being so darn huge (read: not fitting into anything but my winter clothes), I was losing motivation quickly and it was causing more stress than I wanted to live with. I see the leptin reset as a major compliment to hCG, so I'm excited to apply it to my rounds, do it between rounds, and longer term after I'm at a healthier weight. So far, the LR has felt great, but with me having Hashi's, adrenal fatigue, and who knows what else until I get some labs, it could take a very, very long time for the LR to work...and my heart hurts when I think of all the time I'm wasting, wasting my life. HCG has worked beautifully for me in the past (30+ pounds in as many days) and I was one of the "roguers" over on the big hCG forum, although I gained it back on and after a vacation (vacation is my downfall - especially when we visit family for a few weeks in New Orleans - must.stay.away. from the pasta and po' boys!).

Anyway, I'm really excited to have a game plan that works for me now. I feel like I have a clear map to getting healthy and feeling great now. That, in and of itself, feels great.

Hey Meg!

I had already started HCG when CT came to be.. I was also playing with Fat on and off before I started CT. I noticed when I combined fat and CT my losses were super. I haven't had any increased hunger since starting CT either. I also am only doing face dunks and ice packs. (2-3 hrs per day) Lots of surface area.. I don't have a tub, but soon will have a chilly pool I am 'sorta' looking fwd too

The only detox symptoms I noticed were like 2 pimples and my TOM came early while on the pill.. Other then that I haven't noticed anything in a while..

I have been following the Fat Flush HCG thread over at low carb friends.. There are a bunch of people having great success combining fat and hcg.. Take a look.. TOM is arriving with the Easter Bunny so I am boycotting the scale as well for a few days. I can feel I am still losing. Next week I will be done with HCG and moving into the leptin reset + CT.. I am also going to incorporate JUDDD maintenance rules and/or (if I need a correction day- or want to lose more) JUDDD weight loss rules. It seems like the only way to do the reset and still be able to correct, as you can't do a steak day on the reset..

I wish you all feel better! Maybe just stick to face dunks and spot icing while oh HCG? I donno? Maybe I would have more of these symptoms if I had a tub.. Probably...

### rbu5@sssnet.com — 2012-04-10T03:27:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-13199>

nuttmegs, I think Connie stated that you wouldn't need the storage vial if you are mixing the bac water right into the pill vial. You may want to cover the vial once mixed with foil to store in the refrigerator. If you feel better using the storage vial, yes, I would get the amber ones to keep the mixed hcg protected from light. HTh, and if you need any help with mixing lmk.

### colleencoble — 2012-04-10T05:07:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-13233>

You would mix 4 cc of fluid in that vial, meggs. If it's a hucog vial, that fills it clear to the brim. If it's Corian, you will have a bit of room at the top. No need to get a sterile vial unless you want to mix it more dilute. I would start at 30 units which is 150 iu but you may need to go down a bit from there but we'll see.

### Zoe Patricia — 2012-04-11T21:00:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-13846>

Sorry about your aunt Colleen.

Checking in here. I am on day six and cannot get the dose correct. I have been doing CT now since early February and am doing 30 to 45 minutes once a day in ice tub. Round one I lost 28 pounds, I started CT the last week of round one. I did the reset between round one and this round. Followed it carefully with daily CT and gained 5 pounds over the six weeks. This round I am just trying to make it work at all. Here is what info I have. I am taking hucog 2,000 in 8cc bac water. I started by injection 130, then as I was hungry I brought it down to 125 (50 on insulin syringe) then to 120 for 2 days. I did good the first day on 120 but the second day on 120 I was uncomfortable with hunger late in the day and could not sleep. This morning I went back up to 123.

The CT definitely reacts for me with the HCG. I am sensitive enough to get that big time. I seem great before the CT, the hunger comes like a storm after the CT.

Tomorrow is my skip day. Should I skip? Then what dose? I am aiming for 30 days and I am not in harmony yet. My gut feeling is to stay at 120 and split into 2 injections and not skip. I am hoping for 30 days.

I am considering 2 injections a day because I feel completely different before and after the afternoon CT.

I keep thinking after the CT , hey what happened to the hcg that was on board... HCG is supposed to have a 33 hour half life but wow it really feels to me like the CT burns it off. It feels like I am just on low calorie with no hcg.

### jeepifer@gmail.com — 2012-04-12T02:21:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-13997>

Need some help!

Started HCG SubQ injections on Saturday, today was VLCD3. Dose @ 175 iu Hucog.

I am eating no fruit or melba. Food looks like this:

BAB: 2 servings protein, 1 serving veggie

LL (late lunch, trying to stretch to dinner only eventually): 1 protein, 1/2 veg

DIN: 1 protein, 1/2 veg to bring me to 500 cals (no less than 450)

My problem is approx 2. hours after the LL, I start having \*intense\* cravings. I mean -- wanting to dip my kid's Cheez Its into ranch dressing, snarf the jelly beans from easter, and polish it off with a chocolate bar -- kind of cravings. All foods I don't care for, pass up all the time, no big deal.

I am not hungry the rest of the day, except for the, "Hmm, I think it's time to eat some lunch now." type, which isn't abnormal. I'm fine after eating. It just seems to be about 3:30-4pm in the afternoon.

SO what is causing this? It's only been these 3 VLCD days... and it doesn't seem to pass if I eat an early dinner. I though perhaps I was just hungry. Any ideas??? It's truly driving me nuts. I nearly face-planted into the food trough this afternoon, it was a close, close, close call. I can't go 40 more days battling this!

### colleencoble — 2012-04-12T06:41:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-14099>

Thanks for the hugs, friends! I'm still in Phoenix, heading home on Friday.

Zoe, I would still skip. Since this is not your first round, it's possible you have to do down to about 110 or so. Maybe not but that's a surefire way to find out!

shinegirl, it's impossible to say if you'll have a problem with the leptin reset, but one thing I've seen is that those who don't eat eggs have a better chance at not gaining at the beginning. I'm not sure if that's because eggs are inflammatory or if there are some underlying egg sensitivity issues. But try at least the first week of P3 without eggs if you want to give yourself the best possible chance.

That's a high dose, Jeepifer. I'd skip your shot tomorrow and see how you feel. I bet you'll start feeling great by evening. I never like to see people start higher than 150. And even then, many need to take it down a line or so on the syringe.

### colleencoble — 2012-04-14T01:26:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-14939>

Zoe, if you mixed it with 8 cc of fluid, 25 is only 75 iu. You're taking WAY too little. Are you sure you mixed 8 cc in the 2000 iu? 25 is usually 125 iu if mixed with 4 cc.

Justfourme, I don't know what is happening yet with the CT. Doc has said the hcg sets the body up to expect cold so when we do CTing it should help with losses and help cement them. But there seems to be some problems with some. Not sure what it's all about yet. It's too soon to know.

I always add 25 of air into the vial and then, withdraw 25 from it to inject. Yesterday, it seemed like there wasn't enough and there was air at the top closer to the needle so I keep withdrawing more and then pushed it down. I was nervous that I was doing it wrong.

I am going to be strict on this protocol and I am not adding any experimental coconut oil or anything else. No fats, no oils for the round, which is will probably be around 28 days since I am going to go right through my next TOM.

### Zoe Patricia — 2012-04-16T17:58:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-15592>

This past week, even with all my problems getting started, my shape has changed. I had the unfortunate shape of a water tower. Now getting more feminine. It is a marvel. This is happening rapidly with HCG and at least 30 minutes a day in ice tub. I did not get this kind of shape shifting with just CT and paleo. Or with years of personal training and primal eating. I have done CT and paleo almost daily since early February. Also because of hunger and adjusting to the HCG I have not gotten the 500 calorie food protocol yet at all so it has been like hcg and paleo and CT....

I am hoping to get that piece in place this week.

It took me every bit of a week last round to get the dose right. Yesterday at 2,000 in 8 bac water, I dosed at 35. I was hungry late evening but not crazy. This morning I bumped it up to 40. (120) I sailed through yesterday and so far today I feel great. When I start to feel unbalanced fats help a lot. I sure am tempted to just add some fat. Maybe a small can of vital choice salmon.

I have a low tolerance for hunger , a souvenir of anorexia I guess even though I have been recovered 30 years. I did well last round once I got the dose right (thank you Coleen) and I have kept it off. I am pretty sure the dose is right now.

I know you were talking about exercise . . . but I've just decided that my new theory is that CT is going to work kind of the same way as exercise with respect to the HCG Diet . . y I think the official rule is that whatever you are used to with exercise is fine to do while on the HCG Diet . . but you should not try to do stuff you are not used to in terms of exercise while on the HCG Diet (and I think practically speaking I've found that the basic activlities are fine to do sure enough . . . but I have to take it down a notch or even several, when I am on the HCG Diet . . .
>
>
>
> I think maybe what is going to work best with the HCG Diet and CT . . is for people to already been doing CT . . and to have achieved a certain level of cold adaptation prior to starting the HCG Diet . . then to continue with CT . . . but understand that it might be necessary to dial it back a bit on the intensity of the cold (perhaps also the duration and frequency) because I really believe that it is ture that the HCG Diet makes you more sensitive to the cold . . so it might not be the time in which you are able to do the best in therm of withstanding CT . . (although withstanding and deriving benefits are not exactly the same thing).
>
>
>
> Has anyone started the HCG Diet already pre-cold adapted . . or if not actually cold adapted at least having had a certain amount of CT under their belt prior to starting a round of the HCG Diet?

Hey August!

I started CT during my HCG (p2) around the same time I began experimenting with fat. I also exercise, nothing crazy (down several notches).. Mostly walking on incline and some weigh training classes. I only have done face dunking and spot icing (as much surface area as I can with my handy water filled seat cushions). (no tub here)

You touch on something that I noticed during my p2.. Once I started CT I no longer had the HCG GOOD GOD I AM FREEZING FEELING!' Usually while on HCG I sleep in all the clothes I can find and still shiver.. This time I was in shorts and tank top sweating? I did have much better losses and 'look' a hell of a lot healthier then I did last year at the same weight..

I was actually feeling "lighter" due to coming off my period but having to eat so much the last few days has left me feeling bloated and gross. ### MaggieC — 2012-04-18T22:09:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-16329>

I think Simeon's focus on loading is because he had a lot of patients that had been on low fat diets before they came to him. A lot of us have a high fat focus (now that we know better), and the loading isn't nearly as important for us. I eat a diet 65% fat, and yes, I still load. Not to the extent that I used to though.

### LinD — 2012-04-19T05:06:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-16464>

> **colleencoble;15555 wrote:** I'd try the CT with hcg and see how you do. That's what I'm going to do.

Colleen, I thought you were done and not doing any more hCG?

Since about January, about six weeks after ending the second round, I am up almost 6# from LIW. I wonder if I went too low?... 5'7" & 126 LIW... ?? After all, the second round was more of an experiment to see if my back pain would go away like it did with the first (long) round (it did!) and resolve a suspected MS symptom (it did not). If I lost more on the second short round (30 days), that would work for me since I wanted to lose a bit more. So I did and really liked the result.

I've been doing CT since about Feb, I believe. Now, our Texas water is quite warm and I still haven't been able to get it to 50/55, but do what I can. Spot icing here and there... I find I sleep better when I start with a frozen pack under my chin/neck area at night. LOL. I'm so strange these days!

Since I'm long waisted, I can easily hide the weight so concerned this gain, if it increases, will require another round of hCG before long. Then I wonder if I have mental issues, but I enjoyed 126#. Hard to describe why, but it felt like such an accomplishment and felt better.

I've been eating protein and more protein, plus about 14 pieces of macadamia nuts... Then I discovered 99% chocolate. Rarely any veggies. I mean rarely. Guess it's the time of year to start veggies again... But with the weight gains, I'm hesitant to do so. I had a fruit on Saturday, so that was the first tine since December? I forget when I started the winter eating plan Kruse has been teaching us.

So for now, I'll see how it goes.... My neurologist was unwilling late last year to order some of the labs to optimize my health, so not sure who to see at this point for labs.

But I would do hCG again. It's probably too soon at the moment, plus DH might have me committed if I do. He likes nee the eat I am, but he liked me before I lost 40+#.

Following this thread to see how y'all are doing. (oh, and CT is addictive!)

Sprint Samsung Epic

### colleencoble — 2012-04-20T04:29:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-16884>

You could skip anytime after you have had 4 shots. This is your first VLCD though so I would wait another day and see how you do. If you'd had hunger earlier in the day, I would be suspicious but I think it's just the first day minor twinges.

I shouldn't lose slower. That's really kind of a fallacy with hcg. Most people I see on the protocol who want to lose 15 pounds lost them in 2 weeks. Not to lose a pound on the very first day is very suspicious. The scale could be tricking me tonight so we'll see what tomorrow brings. And even if I don't lose, I'll give it a few more days before I conclude anything.

### colleencoble — 2012-04-21T18:48:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-17450>

At one point he suggested rather than coconut oil, we try butter or cream. I don't believe the hcg diet and dairy don't go together. It's the FAT that doesn't work. The protocol allows 1T of milk. And the vegetarians on the protocol often use whey protein or yogurt and low fat cottage cheese and lose.

I just tried it as an experiment for Doc. I am the type of person the protocol was written for so if it worked for me, it would work for just about anyone. It's not working. CT has not made me able to add fat. I will try coconut oil but I doubt that will change anything. Those of who who are the prime patients for hcg do not do well on fats + hcg. There are some it will work for but it's like everything on the protocol--Dr Simeon tested all kinds of different foods and chose what worked for everyone. he left off things that worked for some and not for others. Fat is one of those things. I wish CT would have changed that. I hoped not to have to give up my cream like I have in other rounds, but I will do it.

I will try CT + hcg and strict protocol after I try coconut and see if that increases losses.

i cant remember where - just go to his blog and search for hcg - but in one of his reports, he wrote that HCG + Coconut OIL do not go together...something about the cold therapy and tropical foods not mixing....seems to me if one was to add fats + hcg they'd do better with Lard? or something to that effect.

are you sure you are not hyper sensitive to butter/cream? i know i'm supposed to avoid all dairy bc of hashi's but sometimes a splash of heavy cream here or there does not outwardly affect me (no bad symptons which i can detect)...however, perhaps it still affects me even in small amounts on some tiny level? maybe it does for you as well and the result is a stall in hcg effectivness? perhaps you'd do better trying lard instead...not sure, just throwing ideas out edited to say : but NOT lard in your coffee, haha

in general i avoid dairy but will have a pat of butter here or there (not right now of course!) and i only splurge on high quality ice cream when it's worth it...for instance, i skipped a scoop of ice cream while at an ice cream parlar a while back bc there is nothing special about their ice cream...however a few weeks ago, someone had some of Jenny's Salted Caramel icecream and you bet i tried it (grassfed milk, high quality natural ingrediants)...and when i make a trip to italy, i'm certainly trying the gelato! i hear the coconut flavor is devine....so ice cream is something i only try about 2-3 times a year. i can live with that

### colleencoble — 2012-04-22T01:55:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-17596>

I'm a little bummed. I really hoped it would work. I've been very faithful with CT too. Started the pool at my daughter's back when we talked by phone in early Feb. Then daily swam in 60 degree water for 30 minutes every day for 3 weeks in Hawaii, came home, and went right back to the 50 degree pool every day until we came home in April. Since I got home, I've been doing soaking in a cold tub with ice. I added spot icing when I started the hcg. So I know I've been doing the CT long enough.

But remember, I'm the person hcg was written for. Long term obesity issues. Nothing else worked and I have never been able to vary the protocol. Major fat stores in the hips all my life. Monday I will move to coconut oil and see if it works unless you have a different idea. If still no losses, I'll go back to strict protocol + CT. I love CT! I'm so warm now. I've always been cold, ever since I can remember. i love feeling numb when I get out which is kind of weird.  so I'll be interested in my losses with CT and no fat. Because it doesn't look like fat will work darn it.

### lisaeh3 — 2012-04-22T02:22:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-17600>

Thank you for your quick response! I am also so pumped to start the reset. I have my labs ready to go after the HCG is out of my system. I have had fibro since I was 20 and now 41. For years my thyroid has been tested but it always comes back within normal range. ### colleencoble — 2012-04-22T02:38:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-17605>

I bet you felt great on hcg, didn't you? It elevates thyroid and most all fibro patients I've talked to report feeling much better. You need thyroid, regardless of what your tests show. They check tsh which tells you nothing.

I would also not eat eggs at first as you start the leptin reset. Most thyroid problems are autoimmune and if you eat eggs, you may regain some.

### lisaeh3 — 2012-04-22T03:48:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-17617>

I have felt fantastic and quit my muscle relaxer for 2 days! That was a bad mistake because I have had a migraine today. I hate taking it because I know it is messing with my liver. Thank you for your advice on no eggs. Also, I read that you have no gallbladder and in previous posts you were having signs of LS. How long did it take and what supplements are/were you taking? I am currently taking probotics/HCL/digestive enzymes. I tried bile salts and it caused me to have stomach issues.

I am looking forward to the reset and wanting to feel good like you feel. I have felt so bad for so many years and have research the web to find out why I have struggle.

In regard to my HCG protocol, the last 2 weeks, I stopped face dunks and cold showers and only spot treating. I was having emotional/health issues with CT so I backed off. I have found that I am losing more and believe that the CT along with HCG was too much for my body. I had breakfast with CO only to give my body enough time to burn the fat. I also ate 3 meals a day. Might be something you can try. I think people that have or in your case had fibro, their body is very sensitive and trying too much will set them off. I have also had problems in the past losing weight and found that I have been very successful on HCG. I have 20 more pounds to lose but want to feel good first and Look Hot 2nd! Good luck to you on your journey and thank you so much for you advice!

### colleencoble — 2012-04-22T04:08:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-17628>

Hcg eating primed me for that. I got rid of my fibro long ago, probably 15 years ago. All my lab work was "normal" but I read the [www.drlowe.com](http://http://www.drlowe.com) site and took it to my doctor and he let me try Cytomel which is direct T3. That cured it. ### challdo — 2012-04-22T19:33:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-17795>

Hey all

Checking in.

I have just completed the first short round doing CT and keto hCG. Only an occasional nibble of lettuce or a single strawberry during this time. Spread out the meat with a couple of ounces in the a.m. to help hold all the hCG recommended supplements. Felt hungry most days but was strong during my weekly horse riding lessons.

I only lost 12 lbs with 20 shots but really lost inches. My body fat in lbs went down 5.5 lbs which worries me about lean mass lost. This wasnt the case in previous rounds. BMI went from 29.5 to 27.5. Body fat % went from 34.7% to 33.8%. This was not my best round.

I have previously lost 55lbs on this diet with the last 20lbs regained-life and bad diet/schedule. I am also a physician who prescribes this diet, as my observations, Phase 3 is the most critical for control. I did a long round that ended in Jan and went right into Leptin Rx/Leaky gut and stabilized with about a 4 lb weight gain only.

CT felt good and made me wake up. Because of my schedule, I would wake, shower, eat then CT and go to work cold. Had a few detox symptoms but not bad.

I am going to test now. Last testing showed hsCRP around 4-so hoping that is down. Rev T3 ratio was 80 which is good. I am on bioidentical hormones-progesterone and testosterone only. Not menopausal yet....

Cow , pig and lamb are coming soon to my freezer, joined a CSA this weekend. Optimal is in sight...

Cindy

### colleencoble — 2012-04-22T19:34:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-17796>

> **nuttmegs17;17837 wrote:** colleen - never asked before - what started the weight gain/obesity issues for you? hashi's? i recall you saying you were quite thin when you were young...i'm just curious about your story

I had a ruptured ovary at 14 and was chubby then. Six months later the other ovary ruptured but the doctor refused to take it out, then the Lord. We babied it along until I had 2 children by 23 and then it was covered with cysts and the size of an orange. The doctor finally removed it along with my uterus. If I knew then what I know now, I would have insisted on being put on thyroid and progesterone but not much was known about hormones back then. The weight gain started then when I went on Premarin.  And it never abated no matter what I did or tried.

Lisa, hcg works even when you are close to your weight goal. Many who want to lose 15 pounds do it their first 2 weeks on the protocol so I don't think it's that I have little to lose. But it was a good guess! That's the way it is with other diets.

Colleen, I stayed with 500 calories, I did drop my dose from 125 iu to 120 and it helped some. I didnt add fat but I did make some fatty stew meat for meals but only ate 500 calories for the day-as you know, this was a very small portion but delicious because I crockpotted it with red wine!

Also dont want to be on Leptin reset for long. Last time hsCRP went from 8 to 4 in 3 weeks, I hope to follow this trend again.

Gonna watch the scale. On my OR days I do Jay Robbs shake with eggs and coconut milk-yum. Tracked my blood sugars, they didnt spike. I, too, love cream and started back to full cream today. Feels great.

### indigogirl — 2012-04-22T22:21:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-17867>

So today is my 2nd day at 500 calories without HCG. I stopped the HCG at 42 days and have lost about 26 lbs including the weight I put on for loading (+ 5 Lbs loading).

I ate 500 calories a day no grissini ever, 1/2 grapefruit OR 1/2 an apple for about the first 5 weeks then completely dropped the fruit.

Every morning I had three slices of Gwaltney 40% reduced fat bacon ( 60 calories). For a week I was moving my parents from their house into a independent living complex and I was so active that I increased my protein a bit and occasionally added a whole apple that day. My losses stalled that week...I only lost 1 1/2 lbs that week.

So Im happy with my losses.

Ive bee spot CTing and doing face dunks intermittently for about 2 weeks. I took my first cold bath yesterday and plan to do another one today.

I live in Coastal SC and our tap water is already Luke warm so,I have to add lots of ice. I am going to NY for a week on Wednesday and looking forward to being able to take cold baths!

I probably won't segue into LRx until I return from NY. I am worried about bkfst timing while away.

I will be very conscious of my eating in NY...only meat, fish, chicken and veggies.

I am scared bout adding back fat on the LRx and would love some reassurance from Colleen and those that have segued from HCG to the LRx!

### colleencoble — 2012-04-27T04:20:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-19225>

It's the extra fat not the CT. If I'm having fat, I'd rather have my coffee the way I like it. LOL And the ones I've talked to who have done it have had my same results with coconut oil. At least most of them. I know ChimpChick did well on her coconut oil but I think she's younger and has a better metabolism than me.

Though CT + hcg without fat doesn't seem to be producing better results either. Some stopped because they got hungry and the others had the same losses. One I talked to today had hoped she wouldn't have to do any steak days and would be more stable than in her other rounds since she teamed it with CT but that hasn't proved to be the case so far in her first week of P3. Her weight has been bouncing around like usual. I should have asked her if she saw different reshaping. I'll do that. I'm hoping for really good reshaping with the cold baths and slow losses but we will see.

Basically I'm beginning to think that hcg doesn't need any help. It affects the hypothalamus all by itself. I actually think I might be losing in this exact same way if I were doing CT +the diet all by itself. I bet the CT would cut the hunger and release the deep fat just like hcg. It seems they might do the same thing and teaming them together does nothing.

But it's all theory. LOL I'll keep trucking along.

someone asks:

he responds:

looks like it was nov 29, 2011

i for one know something has to be happening..i love to eat, there is no way i'd be able to do this if i was starving. i'm not hungy - just bored sometimes haha - so there is no trickery with this. i also realize this diet is different than taking those pills that just reduce appetitie. i'm hoping robb's sign off on this helps my husband some. he told me last night that he doesnt like that fact that i'm taking hcg...he thinks 500 is too little (and he thought i was doing a short round, not a long one) i tried to explain how it works but i'm not very good at it. i just told him, i'm not hungry, i have energy to still exercise, i think its a good way for me to "Cleanse" my body (since its so strict, its def giving my gut time to heal and should help me pin down any more potential problem foods for me)...plus its working! multiple dr's sign off on this, and i'm doing it responsibly. nothing else i've done has been able to get rid of the "odd fat" i acquired as a result of my hashi's.

ps - colleen - dzguan did not recommend a thyroid increase, and are holding off on the estrogen until next month. they just upped my progesterone a bit and split it into an evening dose towards the end of my cycle. not sure how that will help my fatigue during that time of the month or the extreme dry eye i got around that time, but we'll see!!! my primary doc did suggest i up my cytomel by another 2.5 to see how it goes, so i'll do that. if its too much i'll back off. progesterone in general did help a lot this month - i was not bloated, my boobs were not sore so willing to stick with it!

### nuttmegs17 — 2012-05-12T17:23:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-23388>

I got my response back from SugarFree as follows:

stumbled onto this blog:

[http://myhcgstory.blogspot.com/](http://http://myhcgstory.blogspot.com/)

which has this disconcerting info (this is a letter from sugarfree she got)

"Well, the good news is that your fasting insulin and leptin are just

fine; no worries there. The bad news is that you have pretty severe

thyroid hormone resistance -- in other words, you are, despite your

low TSH (which is actually a pretty useless test since it doesn't

measure anything directly, but by inference), very hypothyroid. Worse:

until you get your ferritin and D levels \*way\* up, there's nothing you

can do about it, since any medication will not be properly absorbed or

used.

As for elevated RT3 in HCG patients, I'm not surprised -- but it's not

the HCG causing that problem; rather a different and even worse double

whammy. Here's what's happening:

1: The HCG turns adipose proto-fat cells into full fat cells, just

waiting to be filled. Not good.

2: The Simeons' protocol -- as written -- drives up fasting insulin,

sometimes into hyperinsulinemea, which drives fat storage over the

long haul, once you're no longer starving yourself. Really bad.

3: The sub-starvation level calories, according to every thyroid

medical textbook ever written, WILL, if done over an extended period

of time (more than 2 or 3 weeks), CAUSE thyroid hormone resistance in

the form of high RT3. The worst.

This is what women are doing to themselves (needlessly, as I've proven

in my blog in so many ways), and this is why I started my blog to

begin with. So that they could see, through Simeons' own words, logic,

and science, and a live experiment -- working so well! -- what HCG

doesn't do (open fat cells or help with weight loss in ANY way), and

what it and the protocol as written does do: trash the metabolism,

make insulin resistance worse, make more permanent fat cells, and

promote thyroid hormone resistance.

Then they wonder why they keep gaining back the weight -- and so

easily. It's a horrible downward spiral, and I am so happy that I have

apparently helped some folks to see the light and give this up

forever."

the one thing i'm seeing from cruising her blog is that she was hungry while on hcg...which tells me she wasn't doing it correctly so she was definitely starving her body and her body was clearly telling her that.

### Claudia — 2012-05-18T03:55:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-25075>

> **colleencoble;25149 wrote:** Hmm, not finding it, Claudia. Just tons of articles on progesterone but nothing specific that jumps out for application. I read a few of them but don't see anything specific.

Here's the article link: [http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2011/09/18/oral-progesterone-and-progesterone-cream-complications.aspx](http://http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2011/09/18/oral-progesterone-and-progesterone-cream-complications.aspx)

Progesterone cream must be used cautiously when applied to skin as progesterone is highly fat soluble and will accumulate in fat tissue, thereby disrupting adrenal hormones such as DHEA, cortisol, and testosterone.

Ideally, progesterone cream should NOT be applied to your skin. Instead use mucous epithelial membranes of your labia or rectum. Absorption through these membranes is more complete than through skin, and hormones absorbed through vaginal membranes enter the same pelvic plexus of veins that your ovaries normally empty into.

### colleencoble — 2012-05-20T21:44:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-25779>

You have to be careful what you use there because some of the creams they use can irritate. You could try it but stop if you get sore. I personally think it's not the right way to apply it though. You want your entire body protected. Most of the experts like Uzzi Reiss say not to apply it there unless it's for a particular problem like endometriosis because your breasts aren't protected.

I did read the article. I also read the Uzzi (I think?) hormone book (cant remember; it's been awhile) and he mentioned that you need a different "make up" for different applications. I thought I had read that gels were what should be applied to the areas "down there" (said in my best 50 shades voice).

But I was just going to try it once and see what happened. That's a good idea - those applicators. I was going to wait until my current syringe was almost empty and just use that to apply the last dose, but I bet it won't be long enough.

I will say that this morning I applied the cream to my upper arms only per Colleen's post. Normally, I will just slather it on my whole inside arm (upper and lower). Then I got dressed, cooked breakfast, ate, and then I got very nauseous for over an hour, vomited, and went back to bed. Ugh. What a way to start the week! My gut reaction was that the different application definitely allowed the cream to have more of an effect on me. I will try again tomorrow and see if the same thing happens.

### Claudia — 2012-05-23T04:35:03+0300

I know 2 women on my Hashi's group on Facebook, and of course that isn't a lot, but they both gained a lot of weight from using progesterone cream. One of them is using an Apex supplement that has worked great for her estrogen dominance, much better than the progesterone cream.

I also have a friend who was on progesterone cream and felt sick and discontinued. I don't think it was vomiting or nausea.

Sorry you felt sick and hope it didn't happen again.

I know someone else, but she was in menopause, who loves progesterone cream and slathers it on all the time. She says she feels fabulous.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-57074>

Hi Colleen,

I'm starting day three of HCG tomorrow after starting the paleo diet a month ago (and finding Jack's website just before that), I thought I would give HCG a go... but having read as much as I can here - and its not all of it as I have to go to bed now (its late), I wondered could you please tell me - do I stick to the HCG protocol and not substitute grain for more protein? And do I add in CT or not? Thank you

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-10T00:42:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-57186>

> **Amber Tyrrell;58376 wrote:** Hi Colleen,
>
> I'm starting day three of HCG tomorrow after starting the paleo diet a month ago (and finding Jack's website just before that), I thought I would give HCG a go... but having read as much as I can here - and its not all of it as I have to go to bed now (its late), I wondered could you please tell me - do I stick to the HCG protocol and not substitute grain for more protein? And do I add in CT or not? Thank you

What I've seen is:

1\. CT seems to interfere with some of the HCG magic (loss of hunger). I had a very good first round, and dropped CT. I think if you wanted, I would get into your HCG groove FIRST so you could know if the CT were messing up your hunger signals. Jack will say I'm nuts that CT can only help, but that's not been some N-1 experiences. If it were me, and you are doing something as damaging as only eating 500 calories a day for 4-6 weeks, I'd want to have it be as successful as possible and learn from successes that have traveled the road before you.

Jack is a man, his N-1 is simply different in a major way.

2 - I'm assuming you mean substitute protein for grain? Meaning NO TOAST, NO GRAIN. . .yes. Drop the grain. Don't eat grains. Up your calories to the 500 with more protein.

I know I'm not Colleen - but I though I would throw in a different $.02. Good Luck. Follow the protocol even if it does seem silly.

Thank you Cavemam... you have given great advice and I will follow it. I just finished my first day of the 500 cal diet today, and I am so out of eating grains and fruit that I never even thought of them, so didn't have the grossini or fruit but instead I doubled my protein at both meals. ### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-10T18:19:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-57340>

> **Amber Tyrrell;58575 wrote:** Thank you Cavemam... you have given great advice and I will follow it. I just finished my first day of the 500 cal diet today, and I am so out of eating grains and fruit that I never even thought of them, so didn't have the grossini or fruit but instead I doubled my protein at both meals. I must admit I felt hungry this afternoon - will that start to ease? Already thinking about those BIG ASSED BREAKFASTS!! I really hope this works because its difficult to do. It's murphy's law, but we really don't go out much at night and in the last week I have been invited to 4 partys within the time I'm doing HCG! Damn!

You can do it!!! I had my 4th of July weeklong family vacation and packed all of my food, so damnit, you can't handle a party! (channeling Jack).

Dosing/hunger is the hardest thing to dial in! What HCG are you taking, how much? give details. First 4 days are the hardest - so many things to adjust to. The good part is you have the big weight losses so that motivates.

Colleen is the dosing master, but chances are if you are feeling major hunger/cravings you are taking too much. I can only speak to the subq injections.

Two types of hunger to be aware of. . .

1\. actual physical discomfort in the tummy area, like it's moving around going "hey bitch, what's up with this!"

2\. cravings, wanting to eat!

Lastly, I think you may want to consider adding in either strawberries OR an apple for late meal each day. There are some extra properties these fruits have that assist with all kinds of biological functions - fructose/carbs are NOT the only consideration when it comes to fruit. Your body is going through ALOT- help it how you can and apples have something that help you keep fat burning. If you you are low cal/in ketosis. I did not take supplements, but in retrospect would do it if I did it again. Search Jack's blog - he lays out a list of them.

Hi Cavemam,

YES I can do it!! The first social gathering is tonight - I think I will skip that one though. Have woken with a headache - not a bad one, perhaps detoxing? But I have lost 1.2 kilos (2.4 pounds) so I'm really happy about that.

I'm taking the homeopathic HCG - it was the only one available here in New Zealand that I know of, I'm taking .38 mls 3 x per day before meals.

My hunger was manageable, I was a little grumpy though, which made me realise being on paleo for a month really stabilized my moods too, I hadn't been grumpy for a while. I think it was just the cravings/wanting to eat discomfort. Another thing is I just can't stand black coffee, so after having one cup black, I thought stuff it, I'm putting milk in this one, so I had 2 cups with a little milk.

I'll start having an apple at my evening meal, strawberries are not available now at all, so will have an apple.

Thank you Cavemam!

Hi Colleen, no worries... I am on day 9 now of the VLCD and have substituded the fruit and melba toast for extra protein. I have lost 10 pounds so far - very happy with that. I started to experiment with just face dunking and I did a short face dunk on the night of day 6, then I did a longer face dunk on the next night, then yesterday I was hungry for the first time! So much so that I had a little cheat - I ate some of the delicious paleo seafood chowder that I made for my family (about half a cup). I thought that I will definately not lose after that... but this morning 1 and a half pounds lost, which is more than my usual loss! So was it the face dunking that would cause me to lose more? And I think it was the face dunk that caused the hunger. I think I'll skip the face dunking -not worth feeling hungry.

Hi Cavemam, I'm ok i think - I've lost 12 and a half pounds and I'm on day 13 of vlcd. Thats about a pound a day average so that's good isn't it? I've decided to split my 43 day hhcg in half and do LR and CT for a few weeks in between. I'm very excited about the weight loss - I have lost 20 pounds all up with the paleo i was doing the month before i started hcg.

### colleencoble — 2012-10-21T23:14:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-59519>

Glad you're doing well, Amber! Though I don't recommend skipping both fruits and subbing in protein. I tend to see people struggle more to maintain in P4 when they go too low in carbs during P2 and P3. I think it's that super low carb diet's effect on the thyroid. I don't mind doing it to break a stall, but not for the full time on P2.

I think he actually thought you had eaten fruit and toast in place of protein...I had to read it a couple times cause it didn't make sense to me and then I realized what was meant...course Jack is the king of confusing writing and ramblings so one would think he'd be a great decipherer!

### colleencoble — 2012-10-22T16:17:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-59616>

No Amber. You really need a minimum of 23 days on hcg just like the protocol states. And I see way better stability in people when they do at least 30 days. There is no reason to split it unless you are going to have to eat off protocol 4 or more days. Otherwise, just push on through.

### amber_tyrrell@hotmail.com — 2012-10-23T12:10:01+0300

I'm getting such good results that I think maybe I should just push through now, I was finding it very difficult before but now I've got into the groove of it. Is it not beneficial to give the body a break from it though? My skin is looking very dry (from not having any fat I presume). Can I take a break from eating off protocol for a couple of days? If so, how do I do that? Do I stop taking the drops two days before my planned 2 days off?

### colleencoble — 2012-10-23T17:37:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-59823>

I would just push through. There is no benefit to a break because it gets you out of the groove. Your skin will recover when you get to P3. I did 5 rounds of 45 days so I know what I'm talking about.

Wow that's a lot of days on HCG! Ok. I CAN do it!! I must say it took me at least 10 days to really get into the groove of it and now it just feels normal to eat so little, so I'll follow your advice, I don't like the idea of starting over again. Long round here i come!!!! Thanks Colleen

### colleencoble — 2012-10-24T00:35:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-59902>

### vwaggs — 2012-11-15T16:14:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-64472>

Do you guys know if it is okay to go right into CT after hCG or will I not re-set properly? Also, if I am 5lbs or so away from goal after my 47 day round (skipping one day a week) can I go a bit longer if I am still losing to get those last few lbs off? I went 53 days on my first round to lose the total 28 I needed to lose but I was doing Rx sublingual. I need to lose about the same this round.

### colleencoble — 2012-11-15T23:11:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-64573>

You should stop after about 47 days. Personally, I would not do CT until after P3.

### Charlotte — 2012-11-18T19:16:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-65168>

Hi everybody, it took me almost all sunday to read this thread with little chores inbetween. Like filling up my new whirlpool with water. It came out a perfect 50Â°F. I am so glad the loading days are over as I hate to eat more than I need. But I ate mostly fat, no carbs which I haven't eaten anyway since 3 months. Since then, I have been in ketosis. I am keen on trying the hCG in connection with CT. I need to find out myself, how or if that works. I still think it might for me. If not I stop and put the temperature up and enjoy the snow from my hot tub. This time, I took my measurements to see the loss in cm also. I had no problems going from hCG to Epi-Paleo, even lost another 2 kg, feeling good physically and mentally. Diabetes is history since then. The only changes I will do from last time is lowering the dose (took 1500 i.U. first injection before noticing my error and went down then to 150 i.U. ), having breakfast and another meal later in the day and trying CT. It is so good for the skin. Will see how it goes. Strict Dr. S. protocol, no grain, little fruit or none. I will keep you posted.

### LinD — 2012-11-18T19:31:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-guinea-pigs.304/post-65169>

> **colleencoble;66571 wrote:** You should stop after about 47 days. Personally, I would not do CT until after P3.
