### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-05-22T01:20:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-26128>

SO...I hadn't heard any results from my MRI. I usually go on the old adage, "no news is good news" but I figured let's hear it. So, I called for my results and to my shock, the endo says I actually do have a spot on my pituitary which he believes is a Rathke cleft cyst. He says it is really small and that he actually does not think it is causing my symptoms. But it needs to be watched and if it gets bigger then it has to be drained. YUCK

Guess this gives me serious motivation to get on board with the CT, especially icing the caratoids. Let's see if I can give an n=1 of no more pituitary cyst after CT. But first, labwork and vacation.

So, I still have no real reason for no TOM except leaky gut and pregnenelone steal and I have a cyst, but as the doctor said "It's not a tumor"

Well, I guess it doesn't matter because it doesn't change the game plan of diet and CT.

Hi V!

I've been off grid for awhile. I have mixed feelings on the weights and AF. On the one hand, I know I can't stress my adrenals. And I know that I am not LS yet. I was in a hot spring (I know - don't kill me Dr K - I LOVE hot springs. I did go in the cold water for a 5 min and the air was cold up there in the mountains) and the next day I was sore. Realized it's from hoisting myself out of the pool. SAD But I need my pituitary working and if Dr K says lifting weights will tell my pituitary to work. I gotta try. I'll let you know how it goes.

I just spent 7 glorious days in Colorado. In Denver and Salida. I loved it. I did get up and eat my BAB every day but I def was not strict with the diet. I started out strong but eventually caved. My brother eats gluten free, and he is super fit so he eats what he wants and doesn't have to worry. That meant lots of GF treats around. We also celebrated his birthday. Lots of peer pressure to eat and "be on vacation." I know it's lame but I'm human!

So, now I'm back. Elimination diet starts Saturday. Gotta get back into CT and HIIT.

and Sleep I need that too...

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-02T22:08:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-29093>

Just noticed that I'm filling out my bras once again. I am peeved with myself for gaining weight but happy that at least some of it is going back to "the girls." I think that means that my estrogen level is getting higher? No one has told me which hormone gives you a behind, but I do believe I remember reading the estrogen puts fat in your chest. The rest of my labwork came in the mail today. I posted them here : [http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?1424-Labs-up-for-hacking](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?1424-Labs-up-for-hacking)

The func med doc ordered some food sensitivity tests and I came up high on wheat, eggs, dairy. Chicken and salmon were ok. Total bummer. I went to an acupuncture guy for NAET treatments and spent $$ on clearing egg sensitivity. I guess I really should have waited until after the elimination diet and really worked on leaky gut RX before going. I didn’t realize the whole mechanism behind food allergy being the stuff leaking out of your gut and into the body. Makes sense to stop the leaky before training your body not to attack it.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-05T18:09:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-29556>

Day three elimination diet. So far, I am eating chicken and salmon primarily. I am also eating homemade sauerkraut, bone broth, kefir water, and kombucha. Sat and Sun I had no appetite. Monday I was STARVING. Today I feel pretty full. I did weigh myself Monday because I knew I had to get on the scale at the doctor and I figured better to see the naked pre-eating number than the full dressed and post BAB number. 145.5

Let me just focus on where I am going and not where I am, I guess.

Jack said on fb today “Are you living at the end of your history......or starting to live at the beginning of your time? Think about it some today then adapt if you need too.

My thinking is kind of stuck on the present as being the future. Which is not what I want to create. I have been thinking that when I gain weight, I “won’t be able to lose it.” I have been thinking that I’ll never see TOM again. I was also reading the thread by tweety “Eating Disorder Tendency” and that has got me thinking as well. Some great posts by Souldanzer and others over there. My choice is to keep my eye on the prize and live today as if I have already achieved my objective instead of focusing on what I don’t have right now. I’m gonna stop asking when TOM is going to return and just assume that each and every time I make a healthy choice, I am that much closer. Which means, elimination diet, leaky gut RX, adrenal RX, Leptin RX, sleep, CT, and most importantly keeping reign on my thoughts. I was late for work and totally working up my adrenals over this when I said to myself “Jennifer, is it worth stressing your adrenals because you’ll be 15 minute late?” NO – nothing is worth stressing my adrenals out for. That is my new mantra.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-07T16:21:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-29893>

Day four elimination diet....feeling better every day...sticking with Leptin Rx but I still haven't gotten CT in. And I am unhappy with how late I have been staying up but living life keeps getting in the way! LOL

I'm still no TOM and still up in weight. Although several people have recently told me how good I look. My friend at work who knows all about my weight ups and downs told me she thinks I look better with some weight on (especially in the face). Well, I'm not happy with the belly fat. So I've decided I don't need to lose weight but I need to transform my body comp! I also decided that while doing Leptin Rx and elimination diet that I will also be following the Bulletprof Exec/Better Baby Diet (as close as I can with elminiation diet). I know Dr K and Dave are not fans of fowl, but besides fish and lamb, that's about all that's on the elimination diet meat wise. I also bought a T-Tapp DVD. It's supposed to be good exercise and good for lymph drainage or something. I'll let you know if I like it.

I'm pretty worried about my mom (and dad). She is stuck in poor eating and literally falling apart.  She has migranes, some crazy eye disease that mostly surfers and people who live at the equator get (which her doctor has told her she has to wear sunglasses all the time for), osteoporosis, and now body parts are falling out (prolapsed bladder last year and now her uterus). She is coming to visit for a few days next week and I am going to get her trying some of my foods. She was trying to do BAB but says it makes her want to throw up. Certain foods she can't eat in the morning - like eggs. So she's been eating Speacial K!  I told her, at least please buy a gluten free ceral, so she got Rice Chex. I suggested maybe she could do sweet potatoes or crock pot for breakfast, since I know she likes those. I also suggested she get the book "Sweet Potato Power" and a collagen supplement. You think that would help?

### kathiebu@hotmail.com — 2012-06-07T17:07:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-29902>

I'm glad you are feeling better  What is elimination diet Jennifer? Is it the AI protocol?

I'm glad you are not focussing anymore on weight. What a change of attitude  Body comp is a far better way to measure health than the scale.

I'm sorry to read that about your mother. I hope you'll be able to help her out. Ask her to eat the same way you do for a couple of days so she can see if she feels better, as an experiement. It's normal that the BAB can seem to be too much the first few days. And as long as she doesn't cut back on sugar and eliminate gluten, I'm not sure if supplements could really help...Keep us posted about her please.

Have a nice day

HI Katy! How ya doing? Loving your binge-free-ness!

Elimination diet is for food allergies. It's 28 days of no beef, no pork, no eggs, no dairy, no alcohol, no caffienne, no fun, no gluten, no dating...LOL It has some allowed foods that I won't eat like nuts, nut butter, non-gluten grains. And then some veggies, some fruit. Here is a link to the actual hand out she gave me.

[http://www.functionalmedicine.org/content\_management/files/ifm\_Comp\_Elim\_Diet\_091503.pdf](http://http://www.functionalmedicine.org/content_management/files/ifm_Comp_Elim_Diet_091503.pdf)

Then after 28 days, you add one food in at a time for three days. (one new food eaten for three days before moving to next new food) See if the food gives you a reaction. So, it is pretty much AI and then some.

Thanks for the care and concern for my mom. We'll see how she does when she's here. If I could only get my dad on board, then she would do it. My dad has opposite problems. He has lymphocytic colitis and is allergic to things like lettuce. They did paleo a la Nora Gedgaudas and both did well and lost weight. But my dad didn't feel better because of his weird colitis thing. SO, they fell off  I told them about the 23andme sale, and my mom sounded interested, so I pray they do that and start working on changing their future. My dad's dad is currently on Hospice care with Parkinson's Disease and my mom's mom passed with Alzheimers. Dad's mom has diabetes and mom's dad had MI. Well, at the very least I hope to help my mom work on healing leaky gut and giving up gluten and diet pop! AGHHHH

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-07T22:10:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-29961>

Hmm...stumbled on some articles about Hypothalamic amenorrhea. My brain fog seems to be kind of bad today. I had that issue with feeling full physically but starting to feel hungry chemically (as in mood getting short). I ate a little canned salmon with avocado, sauer kraut and bone broth but now I feel out of it. Can't focus. I tried reading a few article on her website here. They are very interesting to me in regards to my situation of losing weight and not getting TOM. She talks about how your body gets used to your fat cells making estrogen and then your ovaries don't produce as much. When you lose weight, your ovaries need to step up but sometimes they don't. This is where I start getting lost.

She kind of goes both ways with it...she is a safe starch advocate and suggests at least 100g but she also says if you correct leptin signaling, you may regain your hormone balance. Any way, I need to go back and reread some of her stuff when my head is feeling less like it is stuffed with cotton.

[http://www.paleoforwomen.com/how-extremity-can-make-even-the-best-diet-fail/](http://http://www.paleoforwomen.com/how-extremity-can-make-even-the-best-diet-fail/)

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-08T05:47:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-30062>

From the Eating Disorders blog:

> Ghrelin and Agouti both stimulate hunger but in AN, they are uncoupled from their master controller leptin, **while IL-6 levels are quite elevated and fuel the conversion of T4 and T3 to make high levels of reverse T3. This biochemical maneuver completely uncouples the thyroid, and it can no longer work well. When this occurs it directly affects the pulsatile release of GnRH and shuts off the ability to have a menses. It also destroys the LH surge that is normally associated with oocyte release monthly.** It also activates the alpha MSH program that allows them to live long periods on low calorie diets until they die. The protectant mechanisms afforded to calorie restriction are limited because of the uncoupling of IL-6 and leptin to the normal circadian cycles of light and temperature that normally control the genetic expression of these programs.

No idea if this is what's going on with you but that was probably it for me. Do you have high rT3? I don't think you need to have had AN for this "uncoupling maneuver" to occur necessarily. I'd say any source of inflammation could probably do it.

According to the article you posted you could be leptin insensitive/resistant and your brain would interpret that as very low leptin levels (just like AN) despite leptin being high and therefore, no menses.

I'm not sure I know what I'm talking about here  That's how I put it together for myself.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-10T17:07:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-30407>

One week in the elimination diet, although I slipped up yesterday. I was at a festival with friends and kind of forgot and ate read meat. I don't really get the whole avoid red meat part of the diet any way. I don't react to red meat. I think I figured out why Dr K is down on chicken, it has more O6 than grass fed beef. My doctor wanted me to buy this medical food supplement Metagenix Ultra Clear Sustain but it is made with rice flour and rice syrup, so I said no. But I am seriously considering this [http://www.betterbabybook.com/coconut-protein-formula-with-colostrum/](http://http://www.betterbabybook.com/coconut-protein-formula-with-colostrum/)

I just don't want to waste time. If I am doing the elimination diet, I want to get the most healing done

I feel better every day. More happy and I am so glad to be out of that hell of irritation and wanting to kill people. OMG that sucked.

We got into a bit of a discussion on nutrition yesterday and this guy, who is very fit and very athletic was telling me that he is following this Thrive diet. It's basically a vegan diet for athletes. He says he feels better and it sounds like he is doing it gluten free with his largest meal at breakfast. I did notice that he looked thin for him (he's never heavy). He likes this amaranth for breakfast. I guess it's not as bad as some of the other vegan choices out there but ....Well, it seems like he is not eating a lot of food and still doing his running, cycling, racing stuff. He said he is interested in eating optimally and wants to know "why everyone is suddenly gluten intolerant when we've eaten it for centuries." So, I sent him a few links. I'm sure we'll discuss it more. I have no desire to eat like a bird, but well people have to make their own choices I guess.

Or Rhubarb is in season now!

Fruits Lowest in Sugar

•Small Amounts of Lemon or Lime •Rhubarb •Raspberries •Blackberries •Cranberries

Fruits Low to Medium in Sugar

•Strawberries •Casaba Melon •Papaya •Watermelon•Peaches•Nectarines•Blueberries

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-21T19:36:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-32756>

OMG, the bacteria in my gut knows when I'm stressed out? IF this doesn't convince me to avoid stressing out about things, I don't know what will! STESS causes a MESS in my body. No more stress:

Is Your Stress Causing Leaky Gut and Candida?

Did you know what goes on in your gut can dictate your mood, your perspective, and your sense of optimism?

Having earned the title of the second brain, the gut is in constant dialogue with the:

**Everyday stress could cause harmless microbes in your body to become pathogenic! These bacteria will rapidly multiply and mutate, leaving you at risk for an infection.

■Immune system

■Brain and neurological system

■Hormonal system

■Our inner ecology

The connection between the gut and our psychology is a two-way street. On the one hand, gastrointestinal inflammation and infection can contribute to things like depression and brain fog. (1) On the other, mental stress or trauma can cause intestinal permeability or â€œleaky gut.â€￾ (2) (3)As it turns out, psychological stress can do more than influence the integrity of our own cells. Scientists have found that the bacteria living inside of us can actually detect whether or not we feel stress. (4)

When we experience mental, emotional, or physical stress, we release stress hormones like cortisol and norepinephrine.These stress hormones are meant to protect us during potentially dangerous events. They move energy stores into the muscle, increasing our heart rate and our breath. And in the process, cortisol and norepinephrine shut down our digestive system and our immune system.

Stress hormones move our body from digestion to a fight-or-flight response.The bacteria that are normally present in the digestive system can read stress in the body and detect the presence of stress hormones. Researchers have found that usually harmless microbes will suddenly become pathogenic in response to the stress hormones that we release. (5) (6)

When bacteria become pathogenic, they multiply rapidly or mutate, and this often leads to infection. Once this happens, the inner ecology of the gut is thrown out of balance.

Keep in mind that stress not only signals bacteria to multiply and mutate, it also shuts down the digestive system and the immune system. These systems usually protect us from disease. This means that when we experience stress, we are more vulnerable than ever to bacterial overgrowth and infection.**

Once the inner ecology of the gut becomes imbalanced, the door opens for a wide range of health conditions to develop and manifest.

Anytime you change the diet, you change the bacteria.In addition to the unnoticed stressors of daily living, many of us spend a great deal of time feeling stress. Whether this stress involves global catastrophes, family drama, or personal struggles, the body releases the same stress hormones.

These stress hormones can ultimately contribute to a long list of health disorders, including infection from opportunistic bacteria.

As it turns out, the health of the digestive system is central to how good we feel on a daily basis. And our mental wellbeing influences whether or not the bacteria normally present in the digestive tract are able to cause disease.

This is why we have found that one of the best ways to consistently generate health is to eat fermented foods on a daily basis and, if possible, at every meal!

Diet can help to modulate the effects of stress in the body. Especially when the diet: />

1\. Contains plenty of fermented foods. Truly fermented foods have several beneficial strains of bacteria that keep overgrowth and infection in check when the bodyâ€™s defenses are down.

Beneficial bacteria found in fermented foods and probiotic beverages do things like soothe intestinal cells that have become inflamed and gently stimulate the movement of food through the intestinal tract. The multiple functions of beneficial gut bacteria are so important that some scientists refer to our inner ecology as a â€œvirtual organ.â€￾ (7)

2\. Contains foods that support the growth of beneficial bacteria. This means choosing foods that keep the body in a slightly alkaline state.

Foods that create an acidic environment are foods that contribute to inflammation and that trigger an immune response in the body. When the body is already in a state of high alert from outsides stressors, the last thing that you want to do is eat something that creates more work - or more physiological stress.While everyone is different in their response to food, there are some foods that are notoriously pro-inflammatory and best avoided. These are:

■Foods with added sugar or refined sugar.

■Processed foods.

■Foods containing industry byproduct oils, such as canola, safflower, vegetable, soy, and corn oil.

What to Remember Most About This Article:The health of your gut can influence your mood, which is why itâ€™s often referred to as the second brain. Suffering from gastrointestinal inflammation can contribute to issues like depression, and conversely, mental stress can cause a leaky gut. In fact, scientists have discovered that the bacteria in your body can detect when you feel stress!

Stress hormones can trigger your digestion to go into a fight-or-flight response. This can cause normally harmless microbes to become pathogenic in reaction to the stress hormones released by your body - leading to infection. This vicious cycle throws the inner ecology of your digestive system quickly out of balance.

The good news is that you can change your diet to change the bacteria in your body. Supporting your digestive health will support your mood on a day-to-day basis. Start out with:

1.A diet rich in fermented foods and probiotic beverages to support your gut with beneficial bacteria to keep infection at bay.

2.A diet that will keep your body in a slightly alkaline state. Acidic foods are known to cause inflammation and trigger an immune response, creating further physiological stress.

[http://bodyecology.com/articles/is-your-stress-causing-leaky-gut-and-candida](http://http://bodyecology.com/articles/is-your-stress-causing-leaky-gut-and-candida)

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-25T16:08:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-33433>

> **PaleoCowgirl;33543 wrote:** Jennifer, I loved your post on the gut and stress. And, Sarah Wilson's blog rocks! She has some great stuff on adrenal fatigue too! Hope you had a great weekend!

Isn't Sarah the greatest?

Any whoo....not much to report. I started doing these T-Tapp exercised recommended by the bulletproof exec for his wife. It's only 15 minutes, so I think my adrenals can handle it. She also gave a very good explanation of short-waisted vs long-waisted and how each gains weight. I am short-waisted and pack it in my belly. So, pretty much I am never gonna have the teeny tiny waist even if I was rail thin. Add in the fact that I have scoliosis with a pretty significant curve in my Tspine (55+) and another in the Lspine (35), my waist is short. If I could get my spine straight, I'd be at least two inches taller and probably this weight would be fine on me. SIGH, don't think Dr K knows how to straighten spines (without surgery) but you never know.

Here's her claim:

Get the Most Exercise In the Least Time with T-Tapp

The T-Tapp Basic Plus Workout is one of our favorites for fertility–it’s a premier preconception detox workout because the movements are designed to maximize lymphatic circulation. The lymphatic system is one of the body’s primary ways of eliminating toxins. It’s made of lymph nodes and pathways that run throughout the body like blood vessels. These channels are used to eliminating toxins. The lymph system is not pumped by the heart, however, it’s pumped when we move, and that’s where T-Tapp comes in.

Teresa Tapp is a renowned fitness expert who designs her own workouts and has worked with professional models in the fashion industry.

The effects of T-Tapp are outstanding:

Detoxifies the body

Increases insulin sensitivity, which can promote loss of excess weight

Reduces stress

Skyrockets energy levels and mental clarity

Boosts metabolism

Improves cardiovascular function

Helps the brain gain better control over the body (builds neurokinetic function)

Improves your physique

Takes just 15 minutes and can be done anywhere, so it’s convenient and easier to keep up

[http://t-tapp.com/betterbabybook/index.html](http://http://t-tapp.com/betterbabybook/index.html)

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-27T20:02:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-33997>

not much to report. Elimination diet is almost over, then I'm supposed to add things in one at a time for three days at a time but honestly, i have no idea how to tell if I am sensitive to a food or not. I get a lot of bloating and it seems really random (as in my diet doesn't change hugely and some days I am and some days I'm not). So...kind of like I don't know what the point is but I keep going.

I have been super tired lately. I think the days working on the house with my mom were probably too much (for both of us). I've been a little worried that I messed up my adrenals because I've been SO TIRED all day and then catching a second wind at night. BUT, today I decided to skip my matcha green tea and see how I felt and low and behold, I feel like I have more energy without it! IDK, green tea and I still don't get along. I got nothing else really. Been kind of hungry past few days. Keep eating a BAB that I 'think' is really BIG but am hungry at lunch. So I eat. Been hoping to cut back for money reasons. Between working on the house, my family vacations, supplements and co-pays, the expendable income is a little small. Speaking of copays, I was supposed to see new gyn who does BHRT tomorrow but they called with a conflict. I am disappointed. Might look at compounding pharmacy website for a different doc. Also kind of peaved that the functional med doc has not responded to my phone call at all...frustration. I wanna get where I'm going and NOW...LOL

Which foods are you adding back in? I have random days of bloating, too, but I have found when I slowly add things in, testing it for 3 days before adding in anything else, it gives me a good indication of what I do well with and what I don't. I also notice changes in skin and energy, too.

I cut all caffeine in Feb. and don't miss it at all. I think it messes with me more than anything!

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-28T19:40:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-34193>

> **PaleoCowgirl;34205 wrote: Which foods are you adding back in? I have random days of bloating, too, but I have found when I slowly add things in, testing it for 3 days before adding in anything else, it gives me a good indication of what I do well with and what I don't. I also notice changes in skin and energy, too.
>
>
>
> I cut all caffeine in Feb. and don't miss it at all. I think it messes with me more than anything!
>
>
> Thanks for those pointeer, PCG. I need to know what to look for. I am a little worried about adding "one thing at a time for three days." This is not my strong suit. I was not good with this in P3 either. I'm such a "black and white" person. Frustrates me to no end about myself. I've been thinking about this in the back of my mind and I really have some soul searching to do. I know I will add in pork and bacon (DUH!). I've already added in gf beef (which I thought was stupid any way) and today I'm having shrimp for dinner. But there are a few things that I want to add back in that aren't on the leaky gut protocol or the adrenal Rx. Like Coffee (I have three bags of Upgraded coffee from bullet proof exec) and chocolate and then some things that are not AI friendly like eggs and Greek yogurt. I should be able to add the ghee back in. I guess I'll stay off the kerry gold butter. Should I try them (like coffee, chocolate, etc) to see if I react? 'Cause I do not want to do another 28 days of elimination diet later to test them. I think I would be OK with eggs if I could limit how frequently I eat them. I'm the type of person that has like the same meals over and over again for weeks and then don't eat it for months. I wonder is it healthier to vary, for nutrients and so your body doesn't get too lazy?? I go on family vacation on July 7th and lord help me but my family does make me want to eat. First of all, they will be indulging and second of all, some of them are overbearing with advice on my diet (too much meat, are you eating Atkins, didn't they prove that's bad for you in the long run, meat will give you colon cancer, blah blah blah). Any way, I'm finding my body likes it when I eat some carbs. I had japanese sweet potato and some blueberries (with coconut milk and avocado) yesterday and feel less bloated and thinner. So, I think since it's summer any way, I'm going to eat more veggies. Blueberries are in season now and I'm using them to make dressing for my salad and fish. They seem a little better for me than the strawberries. Someone posted the most delicious looking sushi with salmon, avocado, and mango. Gotta try that for sure. But I'm getting a little carried away here! But Saturday will be here soon!
>
>
>
> paulalynn;34330 wrote:** I can SO relate to this Jennifer. I often wonder when this detective work will end or at least result in some major improvement. All in good time I guess

Hi Paula Lynn! I really believe that once you get going, the gains come faster and faster. We'll get there!

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-28T19:50:51+0300

I should be able to add the ghee back in. I guess I'll stay off the kerry gold butter. Should I try them (like coffee, chocolate, etc) to see if I react? 'Cause I do not want to do another 28 days of elimination diet later to test them. I think I would be OK with eggs if I could limit how frequently I eat them. I'm the type of person that has like the same meals over and over again for weeks and then don't eat it for months. I wonder is it healthier to vary, for nutrients and so your body doesn't get too lazy?? I go on family vacation on July 7th and lord help me but my family does make me want to eat. First of all, they will be indulging and second of all, some of them are overbearing with advice on my diet (too much meat, are you eating Atkins, didn't they prove that's bad for you in the long run, meat will give you colon cancer, blah blah blah). Any way, I'm finding my body likes it when I eat some carbs. I had japanese sweet potato and some blueberries (with coconut milk and avocado) yesterday and feel less bloated and thinner. So, I think since it's summer any way, I'm going to eat more veggies. Blueberries are in season now and I'm using them to make dressing for my salad and fish. > Hi Paula Lynn! I really believe that once you get going, the gains come faster and faster. We'll get there!

I had a hard time with that in p3, but I was keto-paleo for so long that I had no trouble just adding one veggie or one fruit. I also did muscle testing and found out that strawberries (I still eat them, bad I know), rhubarb, wheat, oats, yeast (goodbye vinegars and wines), squash, zucchini and bacon, I'm sensitive, too. Instead of bacon, I do uncured side pork and season it with cinnamon and sometimes cocoa powder (not AI I know). I think the thing with coffee and chocolate is the stress on the adrenals, more so than a food sensitivity. As far as eggs go, I've always read people are sensitive to the whites, not the yolk, so you could do yolks only. And, I don't think variety is a huge deal. Peggy the Primal Parent writes about it on her blog. Have you checked her out? She had a lot of sensitivities and realized she needed to eat what worked for her, and in her case, it's all meat, CO, bone broths...no veggies or fruits at all.

Sounds like your family is a lot like mine. What I have found is if I bring a paleo salad and a bowl of fruit to eat, then I feel like I'm not standing out in the crowd for my food choices and it feels indulgent. I just bring a bowl of mixed fruit -- raspberries, blueberries, strawberries, which everyone loves, and I like to make a salad like this smoked shrimp one or the hot bacon and spinach one.

[http://www.primal-palate.com/2011/01/spring-slaw-with-smoked-shrimp.html](http://http://www.primal-palate.com/2011/01/spring-slaw-with-smoked-shrimp.html) (I made this for Easter and all of my aunts asked for the recipe)

[http://www.thepaleogourmet.com/2011/01/warm-spinach-salad-with-bacon-apple-and.html](http://http://www.thepaleogourmet.com/2011/01/warm-spinach-salad-with-bacon-apple-and.html) (I did this for Christmas with hard boiled eggs chopped in there too and no apple and it was a huge hit at Christmas with my non-paleo relatives)

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-29T20:22:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-34418>

Thanks for the recipes PCG...I need a better handle for you! LOL I'll be staying in a cottage in Maine walking distance to the ocean with my family that I love but they drive me nuts too. We are all spread out, so it is heaven to be together but we'll all be sleeping under one roof. I know my one brother and his wife (she eats fake butter and low fat yogurt) will probably look at my eating weird, but my other two brothers and wives are cool. LOL It's gonna be hard for me because I'm the oldest and still not married. My youngest brother got married first and has two children (5 years old and a baby in April). The next youngest got married next and has a 9 month old. Then my brother closest in age to me got married a year ago and had a baby in April as well. SO, my feelings about myself being 40, single and childless are gonna be raw. Or then again, maybe I'll just freaking enjoy myself! HA

Any whoo....I've been keeping myself distracting with a little project I am doing with my friend. I do like to obsess about something, so this at least has nothing to do with food or weight! It does have to do with dreams. We are doing a bit of an experiement which involves me keeping track of my dreams. What I'm learning is that I dream a lot about FOOD. I have had dreams about my brother and I discuss eating gluten free and my other brother on hcg. All this crazy stuff. But I also have been dreaming a lot about work and about the past (when I first moved back to Buffalo). I had this one dream that I was working with this GI doctor again (I worked for him in early 2000s). And low and behold, that day I was with my client at the hospital and I saw this woman who I worked with at the same GI doc. She's working at the hospital now. So weird. Consciousness and time are funny things. You know there are experiments that show that the future can affect the past. Makes me pray that the future optimal me is guiding the current me to make the right choices to get me there....

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-29T20:45:25+0300

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-30T14:08:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-34558>

I was up way too early for a Saturday, like earlier than I get up for a work day. I am not happy with that because I really need my rest. If it wasn't for Leptin Rx I would have stayed in bed half awake. I think I need a serious reality check. I've been pushing myself too much. It started w my mom being here to help me with my house. We worked our tails off. Sun up to Past sun down. And of course now the projects are never ending! Lol. So I've been doing something almost every day that I wasn't out having fun! Last night I was doing wash and ended up lying on floor for a good 15 minutes lacking the desire to get up. I did finally make it into a cold tub!! I was so hot after doing yard work that it was rather easy. I don't know water temp. Did some warm to melt my Epsom salts and then all the way cold. Did 40 min easily. Going to a friends pool today and she says she can't get in unless it's over 80! Lol. I want to do lots of CT this week in prep for my trip to Maine! Ocean water is usually in 60s. We'll be walking distance to beach so I can go every morning while the others sleep. I did ice my carotids in bed last night as well but eventually took them off to sleep. Doing it again now while I watch a DVD. Thinking about going to the farmers market and grocery and store for a shovel. I need to dig some big roots up. Or maybe a day off is warranted.

### vkiernan — 2012-06-30T14:55:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-34563>

Morning Jen. Happy Weekend! I hear ya about not be able to get up. I was vacuuming our boat out yestday, quite the job actually, and I had to lay on the seat and rest cuz my heart was pounding and I was wiped out. After getting off the HC, it sure is hard to do stuff. I am staying off of it though and will just keep CT'ing. I hope your trip to Maine is very fun. Seems like when you go away and can still do the things you need to like CT, etc. it makes the trip just that much more enjoyable. Perhaps one doesn't feel guilty like they've blown a whole week.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-30T17:27:09+0300

Makes me pray that the future optimal me is guiding the current me to make the right choices to get me there....**

**

My name is Amanda, my friends call me Mandy, if you want other handle options, LOL!

Remember when you're with your family that your worth isn't necessarily connected to if you have reproduced or not (at least that's what I'm trying to tell myself right now). You are talented and have other great traits to offer, so don't get down on yourself when you're with your family. More than likely, they are jealous of you because you don't have to deal with whiney kids, dirty diapers, crying in the middle of the night, etc.

The dream thing is really interesting. I wish I could do something like that. I, too, dream about food -- mostly BAD food, quite often. It's like I can't escape it. I also dream about old boyfriends, college days, etc. a lot. Last night, I was a single girl, met a new guy for the first time (it was actually an old flame who is married with a new baby), and we were crazily making out, in between hosting a party for all of our friends...none of whom I knew in real life. My latest dream is dreaming about getting my period. Not really shocking as that's my current obsession...

Jennifer;34718 wrote:** I was up way too early for a Saturday, like earlier than I get up for a work day. I am not happy with that because I really need my rest. If it wasn't for Leptin Rx I would have stayed in bed half awake. I think I need a serious reality check. I've been pushing myself too much. It started w my mom being here to help me with my house. I was so hot after doing yard work that it was rather easy. I don't know water temp. Did some warm to melt my Epsom salts and then all the way cold. Did 40 min easily. Going to a friends pool today and she says she can't get in unless it's over 80! Lol. I want to do lots of CT this week in prep for my trip to Maine! Ocean water is usually in 60s. We'll be walking distance to beach so I can go every morning while the others sleep. I did ice my carotids in bed last night as well but eventually took them off to sleep. Doing it again now while I watch a DVD. Thinking about going to the farmers market and grocery and store for a shovel. I need to dig some big roots up. Or maybe a day off is warranted.

I think a day off is totally warranted! Totally. Take care of yourself! Jealous of you CT in the ocean!!!

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-07-01T16:58:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-34783>

Had a major monkey wrench thrown into my day yesterday and I am afraid I didn't handle it well. My parents live about 6 hours away in Ohio which got pounded by a storm and now they and about a million other people are out of power. Their power isn't projected to come back on until 7/6/12. My parents have a raised ranch and just did a major restoration in the downstairs, which is three bedrooms, full bath and a living room. The foundation had shifted, so it was a total gut job. Any way, even though we discussed a generator last fall, they never bought one. So, I was enjoying a wonderful afternoon at my friend's house at her pool (my kind of heaven) when my dad called. I started searching for a store any where near them that had generators. I was at four hours away from them when I kind of realized that the store would close before they could drive there. So I agreed to buy one in Buffalo and start driving toward them. Any way, this whole situation totally triggered my adrenals. My friends were coming up with alternatives that included me not having to drive but my dad was getting really cranky with me and I was in turn getting cranky with my friends. In the end, what I realized is that my dad and I have the same stress response - we try to act fast without necessarily thinking everything through. And we get really snappy. When I suddenly felt starving, I realized my adrenals had just tripped out. In the end, my parents got to the meeting point way ahead of me and probably had time to buy the freaking generator themselves (the Lowes in that town had some but my dad just wasn't able to take the time to process the whole thing out). And it turned out I was looking at my friend's clock to time it out but the clock was broken! So it was really an hour earlier than I thought! Any way, I get lost in details sometimes.

So, I ended up taking a four hour car ride and got a headache. Not sure if it was from a lot of sun, stress, swimming in the pool or something in the food that we had at my friends. She had hamburgers and I'm sure they weren't grass fed. I also had a few gluten free non-gmo corn chips with my gauc. Something was a definite "no." Today I still have a headache and my eyes are so dry. Accidental gluten?? I can't think of anything I ate that was gluten but maybe cross contamination or something. Plus I didn't get home until after 10:30 pm and then still had all my dishes to do before bed. Today I feel like crap.

The swim day also brought up a lot of issues regarding this belly of mine. I wore my bikini but definitely felt like blah. It totally brought up a lot of fears about eating and not weighing. Like a dummy, I weighed myself a few days ago because I was feeling thin. Scale didn't agree. I know the stress and fear of gaining weight is going to make me gain weight. I am also really afraid of going into this vacation with extra weight on me because I generally gain on vacation. I know I won't be eating keto. What's with the freaking peer pressure to eat carbs! UGH

I'm tired and it's messing up my thoughts. I was driving to work (yes I am working today) and I could have just cried. I want life to SLOW DOWN. It's going too fast. I work a lot. A regular job Monday-Friday (and one weekend day a month) and a side job every other weekend. So, a day off with nothing to do is really a rarity for me and I spent half of the one I got this month driving. I know I am complaining, I go on vacation next week. But I look at my week, and feel like it's gone. I need the world to stop for like a week! Today I'll be working until 7:30 pm.

OK, regrouping. In other news, I went to farmer's market yesterday morning and purchased some goodies. I got 6 eggs for my elimination diet re-introduction. My bloodwork says I'm allergic and I am AI but I did the NAET to clear them and since I did the freaking elimination part, I figured I was test them. But as I have mentioned before, I bloat all the time, and never really have "stomach" problems related to food, so I really don't think I'll be able to tell. I feel like crap but it's probably from yesterday. I highly doubt that this process is going to work for me. I mean, if a food can have an allergic reaction up to three days later, then wouldn't I have to wait a whole three days in between adding foods. It makes no sense to me. Well, I had two eggs this morning. Along with beef/liver meatballs. They were quite good. I used cinnamon and some penzy spice mix (it might have had cocoa in it).

I think I need to go back to using online tools for my BAB or at least weighing my food or something. I ate two eggs and like two larger meatballs. Not sure that was 50 g protein. Got haddock for dinner and salmon in a pouch for lunch with salad with strawberry/olive oil/ACV dressing. I also purchased some side pork, spinach, blueberries and strawberries from farmer's market. I'm eating fruit with avocado and/or coconut milk (ice cream) and with olive oil/vinegar in dressings. I also have been eating sweet potatoes. But I now that I took these things off the "no way" list, I eat less of them. So, I'm just going with it for the summer. Talk to me in the fall about keto, ok? I need a break.

> **vkiernan;34723 wrote: Morning Jen. Happy Weekend! I hear ya about not be able to get up. I was vacuuming our boat out yestday, quite the job actually, and I had to lay on the seat and rest cuz my heart was pounding and I was wiped out. After getting off the HC, it sure is hard to do stuff. I am staying off of it though and will just keep CT'ing. I hope your trip to Maine is very fun. Seems like when you go away and can still do the things you need to like CT, etc. it makes the trip just that much more enjoyable. Perhaps one doesn't feel guilty like they've blown a whole week.
> Hi V! I think it's a good thing you are getting off the HC. I hope it just gets better and better for you.
>
>
>
> PaleoCowgirl;34758 wrote:** My name is Amanda, my friends call me Mandy, if you want other handle options, LOL!
>
>
>
> Remember when you're with your family that your worth isn't necessarily connected to if you have reproduced or not (at least that's what I'm trying to tell myself right now). You are talented and have other great traits to offer, so don't get down on yourself when you're with your family. More than likely, they are jealous of you because you don't have to deal with whiney kids, dirty diapers, crying in the middle of the night, etc. (LOL)The dream thing is really interesting. I wish I could do something like that. I, too, dream about food -- mostly BAD food, quite often. It's like I can't escape it. I also dream about old boyfriends, college days, etc. a lot. Last night, I was a single girl, met a new guy for the first time (it was actually an old flame who is married with a new baby), and we were crazily making out, in between hosting a party for all of our friends...none of whom I knew in real life. My latest dream is dreaming about getting my period. Not really shocking as that's my current obsession...
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> I think a day off is totally warranted! Totally. Take care of yourself! Jealous of you CT in the ocean!!!

HI Mandy! Dreams are a good distraction for me. And yes, I dream about food A LOT. When I was doing hcg I used to dream about eating sugar and then freaking out. Now I am dreaming about my family members eating my diet. Why is eating \*\*\*\* normalized? (I know that's gonna get the \*\*\*\*). I've been listening to books on CD in my car and just finished two by Lynne McTaggart. Very interesting about thoughts and intention and time. One experiement she researched showed the when men try to affect an outcome (say get a coin to come up heads), they are very successful but women actually ended up getting the opposite intention (if they focused on heads, it would be tails)! Doen't that just figure. BUT if a couple focused on an intention, the affect was most strong. So, if you and your hubs both focused on the intention of your hormones being optimal....something to think about. I highly recommend obsessing about this stuff over food or your cycle! LOL

Any way, I still am dreaming about work. And haven't come close to the goal of our little experiment. OH well...keep plugging on.

OK, well this was LONG and I actually need to do some work!

TTYL blog world

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-07-01T23:59:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-34884>

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

and some suggestions:

[http://www.abundancetapestry.com/how-to-love-yourself-in-17-ways/](http://http://www.abundancetapestry.com/how-to-love-yourself-in-17-ways/)

1\. Fall in love with yourself. Think about what makes you You. Just like a flower that needs watering to grow, learn to nurture yourself in every way. Love yourself for all the good that you see and accept your flaws and the fact that you are imperfect. This does not mean that you do not learn to change from your shortcomings; instead, you are being gentle and kind to yourself despite all your â€œflawsâ€. Look in the mirror and fall in love with the reflection that is You.

â€œTo love oneself is the beginning of a life-long romance.â€

Oscar Wilde quotes (Irish Poet, Novelist, Dramatist and Critic, 1854-1900)

2\. Eliminate Self Criticism. Do you often berate yourself over the tiniest thing? Is there a little voice inside your head that often tells you that you are no good because you are stupid or make mistakes. If you find that you criticize yourself often, make an effort to stop the self criticism.

â€œI CAN is 100 times more important than IQ.â€ â€” unknown

3\. Be Kind And Positive. When you start to think kindly and positively about yourself, the love you have for yourself just grows. Make it a habit to praise yourself everyday, while in the front of the mirror. Because of such thoughts, you naturally undertake empowering actions that support your development.

4\. Acknowledge Your Effort. It is not always about winning or coming up tops in everything that you do. Many times, it is the effort that counts! Acknowledge that you have done your best, even if you have failed to produce tangible results.

5\. Let Go Of Worry. Loving yourself requires you to let go of your worry. It is a horrible way to live a life filled with constant worrying. I can attest to that! Worry does not help in any way. It cannot, on its own, make things happen. Only wise actions can! So instead of worrying, spend time thinking about what you can do to help in the situation. If the situation is beyond your control, then make a request to the Universe/God about what you want. Next, surrender your outcome.

â€œThere is only one way to happiness and that is to cease worrying about things which are beyond the power of our will.â€ â€” Epictetus quotes (Greek philosopher associated with the Stoics, AD 55-c.135)

6\. Trust Yourself. Have confidence in your abilities. Know that you have the ability to make important changes for yourself, for as long as you put your heart to it. You can also support yourself by visualizing desired outcomes.

â€œTrust yourself. You know more than you think you do.â€

Benjamin Spock quotes (American Pediatrician and Author, 1903-1998)

7\. Forgive Yourself. If you have made mistakes in the past that had caused you to feel less worthy, then you need to forgive yourself. All of us make mistakes; so there really is no need to beat yourself up over them. Also, if you have been carrying around a baggage of emotional hurt because of a childhood trauma, learn to forgive yourself.

â€œTo forgive is to set a prisoner free and discover that the prisoner was you.â€

Lewis B. Smedes quotes

8\. Be Truthful To Yourself. Loving yourself requires you to be truthful about your own feelings. If you are happy, acknowledge the joy. If you are sad, acknowledge the sorrow. When you are truthful about your feelings, you do not try to lie to yourself or seek to bury your negative emotions. Instead, acknowledging what you feel provides a good guide to what your thoughts are. And as we all know, thoughts can be changed, so that healing and self growth can take place.

9\. Grow Spiritually. When you spend time growing spiritually, loving yourself becomes automatic. You become more peaceful, connected, kind, loving and compassionate. You nurture a mind that grows more beautiful by the day. You naturally love yourself in the process.

10\. Make Positive Affirmations Everyday. Reframe your mind with positive affirmations. For instance, say this to yourself â€œI love and accept myself completely and unconditionally.â€ Read your affirmations out loud several times a day.

11\. Express Gratitude. Express gratitude for the person that you are. For instance, cultivate an appreciation for your strengths and gifts. Also, feel a sense of gratitude that you are alive and well, and fully capable of making a difference in your life.

12\. Nurture Your Dreams. Why deny yourself your dreams? When you nurture your dreams, you would love the life that you are leading. Every moment that you live is a joy because you are expressing yourself fully.

Twenty years from now you will be more disappointed by the things that you didnâ€™t do than by the ones you did do. So throw off the bowlines. Sail away from the safe harbor. Catch the trade winds in your sails. Explore. Dream. Discover. â€”Mark Twain

13\. Boost Your Self Confidence. Make a deliberate attempt to look for opportunities that can help improve your confidence. For instance, if you are particularly good at doing something, set aside more time to indulge and improve your skills on it. Knowing that you have particular gifts can boost your self esteem.

14\. Relax. Give yourself space to take breaks every now and then. If you spend your time working, without paying attention to your health, it also means that you do not love yourself well enough to take care of your own body. Fill your time with silence, soothing music and visions of beauty; anything that nourishes your Soul.

15\. Have Fun. Inject some fun into your life. Life is meant to be an enjoyable. Donâ€™t take life or yourself too seriously. If you can think of life in this manner, you automatically relax and quit worrying over things that do not matter.

16\. Look After Your Body. It is important that you strengthen yourself with proper nutrition and regular exercise. Your body is a temple and you should treat it with respect, love and care. It has been found that the lack of self love is often the root causes of conditions like eating disorders, obesity or even terminal diseases.

â€œTake care of your body. Itâ€™s the only place you have to live.â€

Jim Rohn quotes (American Speaker and Author. He is famous for motivational audio programs for Business and Life. )

17\. Learn To See Beauty. When you learn to see beauty in every thing, you will also see beauty in yourself. Hence, stop to smell the flowers. Notice everything. Feel everything. The pink blush of the flowers in your garden, the greenness of the plains, the whisper of the gentle wind, or the myriad hues of an evening sky.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-02T00:27:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-34892>

Food elimination diets are very hard to do. I did it with 100 percent gf animal protein and fat.... it was easy to eat just that but it was hard to re-introduce correctly. You do have to wait a few days and I'd recommend you eat quite a bit of the test food to actually trigger a nice obvious reaction. I started with the least likely allergic foods just to get a bit of a base back into my diet. My outcome was pretty much don't eat anything that has anything added... not even natural flavors. Protein, fats, veggies, I'm fine with. But they have to be super squeaky clean.

Thanks, Soul. I notice the cleaner I get with food, the more I react to stuff. It's like the difference between throwing something toxic into an already polluted lake or a natural spring. Or that's what I think. I'm on eggs now. Felt pretty bad all day but ducked out of work and went to a one hour meditation and talk and wow...fell a lot better. I needed some spiritual food.

Speaking of, I came across another good article on women's relationship to their body and when you are judging your body or wanting to have a different body, you lose your connection and inner knowing, lose your ability to trust...

Our suffering starts when we don’t feel our body from the inside but as an object looked on through the eyes of others

Feel into your body as it is right now. Your heart is beating loyally. Your cells are so sincerely doing their job. Even if you have an illness, you can be sure that your body is doing its very best to heal. Feel your body from the inside, what a miracle it is. It changes with the seasons and with the cycles of the moon. It is such a perfect organism that with the right tending and loving nourishment will move and grow and love in its own perfect way.

[http://awakeningwomen.com/2011/04/06/the-journey-home-to-beauty/](http://http://awakeningwomen.com/2011/04/06/the-journey-home-to-beauty/)

### chocolate — 2012-07-02T01:24:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-34904>

Jennifer, that is so funny about your Dad wanting you to pull his bacon out of the fire. It just tells me that he thinks you can make everything alright for him, or he needed someone to yell at or something. He must have been frustrated.

One thing I learned from getting older, because I always look to myself to fix things, and apparently blame.... Is to ask the question, " am I being reasonable?", or "is the other person being reasonable?" Or "is there a reasonable solution?" I think I'm probably preaching to the choir and you just had a moment.

When I had my kids, I had to decide my extended family wasn't allowed to have the power to make me a nervous wreck. I shouldn't have waited so long. (I have it to the point that my kids are not allowed to be inconvenienced by my death and I am to be ashes. Of course, they may allow composting of parents by then... and a change of plans is acceptable, if they promise it to be maggot free)

It sucks that so many folks went through all of that. But they are ok. Right? And classically... parents teach you that so many people have it worse. ( That's the crappy part about being human... you find relief in that. I'm the sick type that smiles all day after an earthquake because I'm happy I'm not dead. Honestly. )

I hope you rein things in soon. You have to find a way not to let them yank your chain. (those people that lovingly gave you life)

My dad made a joke that he'd have to buy the gas for the generator somewhere outside of the disaster area that had electric and he hoped they didn't crash because they'd be dead with 10 gallons of gas in the car. Our family has a weird relationship to death. But sis says my brother is with my parents so I guess they are alive. I'm trying to see everything as a teachable moment. And yes, I need to learn to be more chill. Lol.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-02T02:07:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-34908>

Made me think of you and some others.....

> Gastrointestinal problems: Hypothyroidism is a common cause of constipation. Constipation in hypothyroidism may result from diminished motility of the intestines. In some cases, this can lead to intestinal obstruction or abnormal enlargement of the colon.36 Hypothyroidism is also associated with decreased motility in the esophagus, which causes difficulty swallowing, heartburn, indigestion, nausea, or vomiting. Abdominal discomfort, flatulence, and bloating occur in those with small intestinal bacterial growth secondary to poor digestion.31

[http://www.lef.org/protocols/metabolic\_health/thyroid\_regulation\_01.htm](http://http://www.lef.org/protocols/metabolic_health/thyroid_regulation_01.htm)

That's a great article. Thanks I appreciate you sticking here for me. Interesting the bit about cold kicking TRH up.

My poor colon. I should praise god it still works with all the trouble I have given it over the years. I wouldN't be surprised if it was huge. I used to work for GI docs and we had a woman come in with "toxic mega colon". It was really nasty. I had a dermiod cyst the size if a cantaloupe on my left ovary in the 90s. About a week before surgery the thing must have grown and I had partial bowel obstruction. Talk about pain. Of course I didn't tell my doc. Instead I went to the store and bought every laxative on the shelf and a week supply of All Bran.

The poor thing hasn't been the same since. Thyroid def is looking more and more like a Major player for me. Now if I could get the func med doc to return my phone call!

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-02T05:37:49+0300

**Jennifer;34943 wrote:** Had a major monkey wrench thrown into my day yesterday and I am afraid I didn't handle it well. My parents live about 6 hours away in Ohio which got pounded by a storm and now they and about a million other people are out of power. Their power isn't projected to come back on until 7/6/12. My parents have a raised ranch and just did a major restoration in the downstairs, which is three bedrooms, full bath and a living room. The foundation had shifted, so it was a total gut job. Any way, even though we discussed a generator last fall, they never bought one. So, I was enjoying a wonderful afternoon at my friend's house at her pool (my kind of heaven) when my dad called. I started searching for a store any where near them that had generators. I was at four hours away from them when I kind of realized that the store would close before they could drive there. So I agreed to buy one in Buffalo and start driving toward them. Any way, this whole situation totally triggered my adrenals. My friends were coming up with alternatives that included me not having to drive but my dad was getting really cranky with me and I was in turn getting cranky with my friends. In the end, what I realized is that my dad and I have the same stress response - we try to act fast without necessarily thinking everything through. And we get really snappy. When I suddenly felt starving, I realized my adrenals had just tripped out. In the end, my parents got to the meeting point way ahead of me and probably had time to buy the freaking generator themselves (the Lowes in that town had some but my dad just wasn't able to take the time to process the whole thing out). And it turned out I was looking at my friend's clock to time it out but the clock was broken! So it was really an hour earlier than I thought! Any way, I get lost in details sometimes.

So, I ended up taking a four hour car ride and got a headache. Not sure if it was from a lot of sun, stress, swimming in the pool or something in the food that we had at my friends. She had hamburgers and I'm sure they weren't grass fed. I also had a few gluten free non-gmo corn chips with my gauc. Something was a definite "no." Today I still have a headache and my eyes are so dry. Accidental gluten?? I can't think of anything I ate that was gluten but maybe cross contamination or something. Plus I didn't get home until after 10:30 pm and then still had all my dishes to do before bed. Today I feel like crap.

The swim day also brought up a lot of issues regarding this belly of mine. I wore my bikini but definitely felt like blah. It totally brought up a lot of fears about eating and not weighing. Like a dummy, I weighed myself a few days ago because I was feeling thin. Scale didn't agree. I know the stress and fear of gaining weight is going to make me gain weight. I am also really afraid of going into this vacation with extra weight on me because I generally gain on vacation. I know I won't be eating keto. What's with the freaking peer pressure to eat carbs! UGH

I'm tired and it's messing up my thoughts. I was driving to work (yes I am working today) and I could have just cried. I want life to SLOW DOWN. It's going too fast. I work a lot. A regular job Monday-Friday (and one weekend day a month) and a side job every other weekend. So, a day off with nothing to do is really a rarity for me and I spent half of the one I got this month driving. I know I am complaining, I go on vacation next week. But I look at my week, and feel like it's gone. I need the world to stop for like a week! Today I'll be working until 7:30 pm.

OK, regrouping. In other news, I went to farmer's market yesterday morning and purchased some goodies. I got 6 eggs for my elimination diet re-introduction. My bloodwork says I'm allergic and I am AI but I did the NAET to clear them and since I did the freaking elimination part, I figured I was test them. But as I have mentioned before, I bloat all the time, and never really have "stomach" problems related to food, so I really don't think I'll be able to tell. I feel like crap but it's probably from yesterday. I highly doubt that this process is going to work for me. I mean, if a food can have an allergic reaction up to three days later, then wouldn't I have to wait a whole three days in between adding foods. It makes no sense to me. Well, I had two eggs this morning. Along with beef/liver meatballs. They were quite good. I used cinnamon and some penzy spice mix (it might have had cocoa in it).

I think I need to go back to using online tools for my BAB or at least weighing my food or something. I ate two eggs and like two larger meatballs. Not sure that was 50 g protein. Got haddock for dinner and salmon in a pouch for lunch with salad with strawberry/olive oil/ACV dressing. I also purchased some side pork, spinach, blueberries and strawberries from farmer's market. I'm eating fruit with avocado and/or coconut milk (ice cream) and with olive oil/vinegar in dressings. I also have been eating sweet potatoes. But I now that I took these things off the "no way" list, I eat less of them. So, I'm just going with it for the summer. Talk to me in the fall about keto, ok? I need a break.

Hi V! I think it's a good thing you are getting off the HC. I hope it just gets better and better for you.

HI Mandy! Dreams are a good distraction for me. And yes, I dream about food A LOT. When I was doing hcg I used to dream about eating sugar and then freaking out. Now I am dreaming about my family members eating my diet. Why is eating \*\*\*\* normalized? (I know that's gonna get the \*\*\*\*). I've been listening to books on CD in my car and just finished two by Lynne McTaggart. Very interesting about thoughts and intention and time. One experiement she researched showed the when men try to affect an outcome (say get a coin to come up heads), they are very successful but women actually ended up getting the opposite intention (if they focused on heads, it would be tails)! Doen't that just figure. BUT if a couple focused on an intention, the affect was most strong. So, if you and your hubs both focused on the intention of your hormones being optimal....something to think about. I highly recommend obsessing about this stuff over food or your cycle! LOL

Any way, I still am dreaming about work. And haven't come close to the goal of our little experiment. OH well...keep plugging on.

OK, well this was LONG and I actually need to do some work!

TTYL blog world

Sounds like you had a bit of a stressful weekend! I have a hard time when my plans are ruined with the unexpected! Having to drive all that way and dealing with crabby parents is rough. Oddly, I had to leave a wedding in May to buy my parents a generator when their house was flooding and the electricity was out. It lead to some pretty abusive behaviors on my body...but we learn. I'm learning to cope with stress in different ways.

I like what you wrote about spiritual cleansing. I'm going to work on that this week....some healing, detoxing, meditating, walking, breathing, loving. That's my plan.

[http://realfoodsummit.com/](http://http://realfoodsummit.com/)

29 farmers, nutritionists, and bloggers get back to basics and show us how Just Eating Real Food is best for not only our health but the health of the soil, the plants, the animals, and the future of our planet.

•Jeffrey Smith dishes the real dirt on genetically modified foods

•Mark McAfee shows you how raw milk can heal a broken immune system

•Chris Masterjohn shares the nutritional wisdom of Weston A. Price

and more ...

Note to self - the liver detox tea makes you feel like crap.

You think I would learn not to drink it. I had some yesterday at lunch and it just made me slightly woozy. Today I had some with breakfast and hello, I want to kill people. OMG, I can't wait for lunch so I can recover. Pluse my eyes are super dry. Not good.

In the good news department, I'm pretty sure that I can eat eggs. I haven't see any response to them. Although I feel horrible right now, I'm pretty sure it was the tea. I ate the eggs first this morning with my BAB along with liver/beef meatballs and tea last. I was remarking to myself how good I felt. Now, not so much. No more liver tea! Or is it just releasing all that nasty anger? Supposedly you hold anger in the liver.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-03T18:51:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-35301>

Hopefully today will be play day (outdoor concert if it doesn't rain). Now just to decide whether I return the ex's phone call or let it slide....

When I moved to Boulder I was having trouble finding my final living destination for a few weeks and living in chaos and disaster around me (boxes.... trashed b/c I had to pull stuff out all the time). I had a buddhist mentor since I was attending a buddhist university and I was required to sit everyday.... I said the same thing. Can't do it, sorry, chaos around me. SO, my teacher had me sit in the worst spot of the place I was temporarily staying at..... and I had to learn to clear my mind and the chatter in right that spot.

Quite a task! But at the end of the experience I was more able to let go of chaos around me. I still prefer clear, clean and calm environments... but when it's not so I just am in that space. I think it took me the whole first semester to acquire some sort of meditation skill in my chaotic surroundings. But now I can meditate in a freaky MRI scanner that rattles and vibrates and makes the weirdest noises and not be bothered too much.

As for the ex.... no idea  Sounds like it could go either way. Go with your gut?

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-07-03T21:45:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-35331>

[http://www.grassfedgirl.com/one-month-without-a-scale/](http://http://www.grassfedgirl.com/one-month-without-a-scale/)

Confession Time:

I am embarrassed to tell you this because I am supposed to be more evolved (lolâ€¦paleo joke) but I usually weigh myself every few days (if not everyday). My husband went out of town for a trip and before he left he hid my scale from me at my request. I thought it was no big deal at first but then I really started to miss it. I thought I knew where he put it and I even started to look, but I couldnâ€™t find it.. I guess that is a sign that I did not need to subject myself to that torture of weighing.

Are you addicted to the scale?

Why do I feel the need to weigh?

The rational part of me knows that the scale does not really tell me what is going on with my health but the brainwashed â€œUSâ€￾ magazine side of my brain wants to be on that scale every morning. Back in 2009 when I gained 20 lbs in one month and later realized I was hypothyroid and had Hashimotoâ€™s, I became manic about weighing myself. Getting on the scale felt like my only way of holding on when I was on an out of control roller coaster. I am still on a healing journey and have not fully resolved my issues. I am currently working with functional medicine doctors to regulate my thyroid and adrenal glands so they can get back to their optimum function. The thyroid is an endocrine gland that regulates energy levels, digestion, oxygen consumption and mobilizes fat from storage so if it is not working well all of these systems will be affected.

Scale = Stress

Nothing is more stressful than seeing that number on the scale going up (or even staying the same) which will also raise the fat storage hormone called cortisol. If you are dealing with thyroid issues or adrenal fatigue the only way to heal is to reduce your stress. Obsessing about weight daily will only increase your stress hormones and belly fat. Just get some sweatpants and focus on good sleep, restorative exercise and healing nutrition rather than the scale numbers.

One Month without a scale:

Its been four weeks since I weighed myself and you know what happened? My weight stayed the exactly the same. Would I have lost weight if I weighed myself everyday? I doubt it. Would it have caused me a great deal of stress and anxiety each time I weighed? Yes, that is a given. I would have mentally beat myself up assuming I donâ€™t have self control or some other negative line of thinking that only causes more negative feelings. This month without a scale has helped me to focus on healing rather than berating myself daily. For some reason my body is holding on to this weight to protect itself because my thyroid/adrenals are not functioning optimally. It is not because I am a bad person or lacking in personal responsibility. To quote Dr. Janet Lang â€￾ Hormones are more powerful than dietâ€￾. When your body is healthy again it will release the weight so it is up to us to keep looking for that underlying cause and love ourselves in the process.

Glad to know there are fellow travelers on this journey

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-35376>

> **KiwiLauren;35515 wrote:** I've had this exact instruction given to me! It's the perfect antidote to the thinking that goes..."things will be just fine when....". As Pema Chodron likes to say (as Souldanzer knows, she's one of my heros), it's easy when you go up the mountaintop to meditate. The real living is when you get off the mountain. Can you keep your centre then?
>
>
>
> I try to think of the daily pressures and chaos as little reminders to return to this practice... like building a muscle slowly. Then when the pressure is really on, I've got more strength.
>
>
>
> Glad to know there are fellow travelers on this journey

Thanks for sharing that Kiwi. I've heard it said "if you think you're enlightened, go spend the weekend with your parents"! Lol. Yes we need to practice in peace time is what my teacher always says. I feel like I've gotten away from my spiritual roots and I'm not happy w myself on that. Life is such bliss when you're "there" makes you wonder why you let yourself fall back into the suffering traps. Oh well. Guess its all part of the plan. I do think Leptin Reset and CT are both good for spiritual practices. My intuition goes way up when I CT. I think it's true for Dr k too. But I'm just guessing.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-07-04T02:58:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-35396>

I'm so tired. I wanted to go to this fun outdoor concert tonight but it looked like rain. Skies cleared up but my motivation evaporated. Tried so hard to talk myself into going to the store for just a few little things. Even got dressed up decided to cold tub it instead. I need sleep and rest for my vacation! Better rehab the adrenals or I'll never make it with all the babies and grandparent stress! Lol. Don't cry for me, I'm going to the beach!

This morning after BAB I was HOT. Of course its summer. The house said 76. Felt hotter. When I do CT I'm lying on my back in the tub. Kind of bothers my neck because I'm trying to get down In there. I keep my feet on with knees bent so thy are out and they feel hot!

So tired. Gonna do 30 min then bed. Wish I could sleep outside its cool w a breeze. Love that

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-07-05T15:57:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-35612>

booo...my freaking cystic acne is back with my cycle. Just posted an ASK JACK about it. In the past I took minocycline ofr it and that works great, but I'm guessing its no good for my gut to do this???

Why must hormones come with extra weight and face acne?

Can you not have optimal and look good?

Sorry, cranky pants.

I restarted CT and was so proud of myself for staying in 30 minutes until my lazy butt finally remembered to bring the thermometer upstairs. I was just filling eth tub with the faucet all the way cold, well it's only 70 degrees. LOL felt cold Guess I need to get myself some frozen water bottles or something.

I keep forgetting to take my temp in the morning too.

And the bloat. WTF I cannot figure it out. I thought it was the coconut milk so I finally cut it out. Yesterday during the day, bloat not too bad. After dinner, my tum was huge, like I swallowed a beach ball. And I had haddock with CO and lemon, raw zucchini, and some chocolate CO Crack (yes I added in chocolate next!). WTF, none of those are FODMAPs. I am at a total loss.

Any whoo... one day of work and then vacation!

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-05T18:12:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-35642>

Isn't acne an indicator of something being out of whack with the hormones? I rarely can get myself to go anywhere at night.. it get's dark I want to sleep.... I figure one thing that's working in my body no matter how it's affecting my social life.

Same problem with tap water getting too warm.... it took 60lbs of ice to get it down to 45 the other day. I do the frozen bottles but my freezer is so small it's a pain. I think getting another freezer has just moved to priority #1.

I don't have the bloating anymore but I dealt with that during re-feeding (though I'm finding out I might \*still\* be re-feeding ) and it was not pleasant at.all. Painful and sucks and all I wanted to do was lie down and hold my tummy. For me it just went away with time. Sometimes I still bloat a little but that's usually when I think I need to eat a heap of veggies all at once

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-05T18:24:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-35647>

Souldanzer, what do you consider a refeed?

Jennifer, when I did muscle testing for foods, I found out zucchini and squash were a no-no for me. My doc said that's very common. I was eating boatloads of it, too, from my garden and had no clue. Maybe cut it for awhile and see what happens.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-05T18:30:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jennifers-moving-from-leptin-reset-ct-adrenal-rx-leaky-gut-rx-ai-to-optimal.323/post-35650>

My body being in a state of recovering weight that was lost due to starvation. Often co-occurs with hyper-metabolism after AN and requires significant amounts of caloric intake beyond the expected level to achieve necessary weight restauration.
